Another highlight is the incredibly popular and iconic Singaporean chicken rice.

properties.name: Onjium
properties.description: Across the street from the stone wall of Gyeongbokgung Palace, Onjium blends traditional Korean aesthetics with modern architectural calm. Guided by court cuisine specialist Cho Eun-hee and Park Sung-bae, the culinary team reinterprets traditional Korean flavours through modern techniques, seasonal ingredients, and years of research. Each dish is rooted in history yet speaks to today’s palate. Counter seating offers a glimpse into the quiet focus of the kitchen, while carefully curated beverage pairings enhance the experience.

properties.name: Onyva
properties.cuisine: B1, 15 Dosan-daero 75-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06015, South Korea
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet alley, Onyva embodies the chef’s vision of a relaxed, welcoming dining space. While rooted in French cuisine, the restaurant’s menu creatively incorporates Korean ingredients to present distinctive flavours. A standout is the buckwheat galette filled with grilled eel, crispy ginger, and caramelised onions for exceptional texture and depth. The six-course tasting menu is the standard offering, but à la carte options paired with wine are available at more accessible prices after 9 p.m.

properties.name: OPNNG
properties.cuisine: B1, 22 Dosan-daero 34-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06049, South Korea
properties.description: At OPNNG, delight your taste buds with hard-to-find rare wines while relishing the overall vibe. The name symbolizes “the opening up of new taste vistas through gastronomy and wine.” Resembling more of a gallery than a wine bar or restaurant, this fine dining venue impresses patrons with an arch-shaped entrance, paintings by renowned artists, and a vast wine cellar that plays host to more than 500 different types of wine. It offers handmade pasta dishes and refined à la carte items.

properties.name: Oreno Ramen
properties.description: Oreno Ramen blends its broth just before serving, creating a frothy soup that pleasantly coats the palate. The paitan, made by boiling a whole chicken, is a milky broth with a nutty flavour and creamy texture. House-made noodles, melt-in-your-mouth chicken, kelp, and egg complete an exceptional bowl. For an extra kick, opt for the spicy broth, and take advantage of complimentary refills of noodles, rice, and broth. Though the restaurant often has a long queue, turnover is quick, so waits are usually short. A side of chilli peppers is also recommended and provided free of charge.

properties.name: Original Numbers
properties.description: Original Numbers offers refined dining at a fair price under the slogan ‘reasonable dining’. Grounded in French technique, the cuisine blends seasonal ingredients with umami-rich sauces such as sabayon and beurre blanc, reflecting contemporary trends and the chef’s creativity. The dishes unfold like a musical narrative, delivering a coherent and engaging gastronomic journey, while the antique-inspired interior further reinforces the restaurant’s immersive atmosphere.

properties.name: Osteria Orzo
properties.description: Osteria Orzo continues to offer an authentic taste of a casual and cozy Italian osteria at its Hannam-dong location. Signature offerings on the menu include the white ragù pasta which features home-made fresh pasta drenched in rich meat sauce with mascarpone cheese and truffles. The spicy tagliatelle and the seafood tartare are also among the restaurant's all-time favorites. Reserve a table across from the open kitchen and watch the chefs in action.

properties.name: Palais de Chine
properties.cuisine: 6F L'Escape Hotel, 67 Toegye-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul, 04529, South Korea
properties.description: Located inside the Parisian Belle Époque-inspired L'Escape Hotel, Palais de Chine is the only space within the hotel that offers Eastern tastes and sensibilities. Inspired by designs from Shanghai's own belle époque, the modern space offers both authentic and modern interpretations of Chinese cuisine. There is a good range of dishes on the menu that stay true to their regional eccentricities, including the dim sum selection. Palais de Chine prides itself on its Peking duck, another celebrated Chinese dish with regional distinctiveness.

properties.name: PERIGEE
properties.description: Perigee means the point in the orbit of a satellite at which it is closest to the Earth. The restaurant was named in the hope of bringing diners closer to food and wine. The restaurant, which is run by chef couple Shin Ga-young and Lim Hong-keun, serves pasta that is all made fresh in-house. Diners can enjoy different types of pastas with textures and shapes rarely found in Korea, such as cavatelli, andarinos, and pici. Experience familiar yet unique combinations like vongole with pici, and lobster pasta with andarinos.

properties.name: Pierre Gagnaire
properties.cuisine: 35F Lotte Hotel, 30 Eulji-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul, 04533, South Korea
properties.description: Internationally celebrated French Chef Pierre Gagnaire opened his eponymous contemporary fine dining restaurant on top of Lotte Hotel’s New Wing in 2008. The elegant dining space, adorned with Murano chandeliers and gold trim walls, exudes classic French luxury. The artfully presented food, made with 80% local ingredients, is respectful of the Paris-based chef’s creative style. With more than 250 labels, the wine cellar display also impresses.

properties.name: Pildong Myeonok
properties.description: Located near Namsangol Hanok Village by Chungmuro subway station, this restaurant is a Pil-dong landmark that has been serving Pyeongyang cold buckwheat noodles for decades. The restaurant’s chilled broth has a delicate beef flavor, its subtlety both loved and disputed among cold buckwheat noodle aficionados. Another highlight of the restaurant is the buttery and tender boiled pork slices, served in thick slices with a dipping sauce. Homemade dumplings are also popular.

properties.name: Pyeongyang Myeonok
properties.description: Pyeongyang Myeonok is a historic restaurant in Seoul that has served Pyongyang naengmyeon for three generations. Expect a long wait on a hot summer’s day. The broth is exceptionally clear with an understated meaty flavor; it is clean and mellow, and the fragrant noodles are full of buckwheat flavor. The mandu, that is stuffed full of different ingredients except kimchi, and the thinly sliced suyuk are also fan favorites. Do note that they have been closing every Monday since January 2025.

properties.name: Restaurant Allen
properties.address: 2F Center field EAST E205, 231 Teheran-ro, Gangnam-gu, Seoul
properties.cuisine: 2F Center field EAST E205, 231 Teheran-ro, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06142, South Korea
properties.description: At Restaurant Allen, Chef Hyun-min Suh’s sensibility shines through in contemporary dishes accented with light French touches. The elegant yet comfortable dining room, paired with carefully chosen tableware and views into the orderly kitchen, enhances the experience. The cuisine is delicately constructed around seasonal local ingredients and reinterpreted throughout the year. A sourcing map in the menu highlights the origins and seasonality of the products used. The makgeolli-yeast sourdough, with its crisp crust and balanced acidity, is a standout. Warm, polished service brings the meal to a graceful close.
properties.address_ko: 강남구 테헤란로 231, 센터필드 East 2층 E205호, Seoul

properties.name: JUEUN
properties.cuisine: 8F Gyeonghuidang, 36 Gyeonghuigung-gil, Jongno-gu, Seoul, 03175, South Korea
properties.description: JUEUN offers a delicate expression of Korea’s four seasons, favouring a classic, understated approach over trends or ornamentation. A variety of fermented sauces form the backbone of the menu, enhancing ingredients with natural depth and harmony, while the seasonally changing courses provide a strong incentive for return visits. The meal follows a thoughtful progression and concludes with a traditional Korean table that highlights a wide spectrum of flavours. Ceramics by local artists and subtle media art create a space where tradition and modernity coexist with quiet elegance. The curated pairings of traditional Korean spirits and wine further elevate the experience.

properties.name: Restaurant OY
properties.cuisine: B1F, 48-12 Seolleung-ro 148-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06064, South Korea
properties.description: Tucked away on a quiet residential street, Restaurant OY blends seamlessly into its surroundings with a modest, serene façade. Run by chef duo Sehun Oh, a veteran of French kitchens, and Le Cordon Bleu-trained Ah-young Yoon, the restaurant offers elegantly restrained French cuisine. Oh handles the savoury courses while Yoon crafts the desserts. Their deep understanding of ingredients and polished technique result in dishes that are simple, balanced, and refined, such as a delicate bean soup, tender pork tenderloin with a rich sauce, and a decadent fondant au chocolat.

properties.name: Rialto
properties.description: Over the past few years, a streamlined selection of antipasti paired with a stronger focus on primi has become a clear trend in Seoul’s Italian dining scene. In keeping with this shift, the restaurant presents a concise menu that stays true to its northern Italian roots as well as the chef’s personal philosophy, sensibility, and style. Standout dishes include tajarin and cacio e pepe, richly flavoured and marked by a beguiling harmony of butter and cheese. The kitchen’s other pasta dishes show the same fine balance of sauces and seasonings. The pickled ponytail radish is another highlight, offering a nostalgic note that lingers on the palate.

properties.name: RMW Carne
properties.description: RMW Carne, which is located on the second floor of a renovated old house, isn’t particularly spacious, but you can enjoy your food in a comfortably petite, cozy venue. The restaurant which is named after the first letters of steak doneness - rare, medium, and well-done - dry ages various cuts of beef of a variety of different cattle breeds. Salami, chorizo, pastrami, sausages and other charcuterie, all cured in-house, are available for purchase at the store inside the restaurant. They also serve simpler dishes like grilled vegetables and pasta that can be enjoyed as a light meal or refreshment.

properties.name: Ryunique
properties.description: Since opening in 2011, Ryunique has continually redefined its cuisine through Chef Tae-hwan Ryu’s independent research and creative interpretation of ingredients. The menu evolves with the seasons and solar terms, highlighting fresh produce selected for peak quality. The chef's ability to translate boundless culinary ideas into refined dishes defines the restaurant’s identity. The tidy, modern space houses both the chef’s research centre on the first floor and the dining room above. Altogether, Ryunique offers a distinctive experience that feels both charming and inventive.

properties.name: Samcheongdong Sujebi
properties.description: This local haunt has been an integral part of Samcheong-dong’s history since 1982. The signature dish here is sujebi — rustic hand-pulled dough soup served in a savory anchovy broth. The restaurant is also known for its potato pancakes, made with grated potatoes that are pan-fried until crispy on the outside and pleasantly chewy on the inside. Avoid the lunch rush if you don’t want to stand in line, but be warned — the restaurant is always busy.

properties.name: Samwon Garden
properties.description: For several decades, Samwon Garden has transformed Korean barbecue culture and enjoyed a reputation for its intricate balance between comfort and haute cuisine. Diverse cuts of prime Korean beef meet the staff’s seasoned grilling skills, creating a delightful gastronomic experience. What's more, a gorgeously landscaped outdoor garden, coupled with high ceilings and glass walls infused with the Korean aesthetics, adds a feeling of spaciousness. Visit this culinary landmark and enjoy a meal loaded with great flavours.

properties.name: SAN
properties.cuisine: B1, 6 Eonju-ro 168-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06020, South Korea
properties.description: SAN blends modern cuisine with a creative flair. The flavours of seasonal ingredients are highlighted and paired with sauces to create a balance of complexity and restraint. Lobster jeotgal and chamoe dongchimi are fine examples of Chef Seong-hyun Jo's imagination. Milmyeon and dweji-gukbap deliver the robust flavours of Korean cuisine, elevated by the chef’s unique interpretation. Quiet service and thoughtful wine pairings add to the dining experience, which is further enhanced by the forest ambience of the space.

properties.name: Sanro
properties.description: Chef Sung-yup Yoo’s dedication to washoku cuisine shines through at Sanro. The nuanced, chic objets d’art that adorn the space are in harmony with the chef’s sensibilities expressed through both his menu and the dining environment. When seasonal ingredients meet his refined technique and passion for culinary craft, a succession of subtle flavours unfolds, gently pleasing the palate throughout the meal. To complete the experience, the service is as polished as the clean, elegant flavours that define his washoku cuisine.

properties.name: Sarukame
properties.description: Sarukame is currently run by its second-generation owner-chef Hiroto Honma. He is carrying on its culinary legacy by preserving its original recipes. Such effort, coupled with his gastronomic sensibility and international experience in the restaurant business, is adding new narrative layers to this establishment. Sarukame continues to create exclusive menus that combine the flavours of trendy ramen in Japan, classic flavours, and new original flavours, thereby shaping its brand identity and serving the ever-changing needs of Seoul's ramen aficionados.

properties.name: Seokyonanmyunbang
properties.description: Chef Nak-young Kim, an expert on Italian cuisine, offers his unique take on noodle dishes at Seokyonanmyunbang. His nanmyeon is a unique, creative fusion of Korean and Italian culinary traditions. The chef delicately balances these two distinctive traditions to highlight the texture of noodles and the delectable flavours of broth. Signature dishes include gueomdak nanmyeon made from a Jeju breed of chicken and seokyo nanmyeon featuring a broth made from chicken and Hanwoo beef. The chef creates seasonal noodle dishes, making it worthwhile to make multiple visits for new experiences.

properties.name: Seoryung
properties.description: Seoryung began its journey in Hongcheon, moved via Ganghwa, and finally settled in Namdaemun, Seoul. The restaurant has drawn noodle lovers wherever it relocated and continued to expand its connection with diners by preparing authentic noodles and broths with dedication. All of its noodles are made purely from buckwheat sourced and prepared by Chef Jong-moon Jeong. This is the secret to the fragrant buckwheat flavour that shines through in all its noodle dishes. The mandu and jeyuk, made from the most tender part of the pork shoulder, provide an even more satisfying experience.

properties.name: Seoul Dining
properties.cuisine: 2F, Designhouse, 272 Dongho-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul, 04617, South Korea
properties.description: Guided by the vision of presenting a culinary journey that captures the essence of Seoul, the establishment combines seasonal ingredients with boundless creativity to bring brilliant, one-of-a-kind dishes to the table. The use of ingredients and sauces that deliver a wide variety of flavours adds to its appeal, reflecting the co-existence of diverse food cultures in Seoul. The contemporary and vibrant dining space also creates a sense of harmony worthy of its buoyant dishes.

properties.name: SIIO
properties.description: At SIIO, the head chef’s experience is seen in imaginative dishes featuring seasonal Korean ingredients, ready to delight diners with sumptuous flavors. SIIO’s fare breaks free from the mold and embraces freedom of expression. At this fine diner, novel dishes represent a melting pot of culinary cultures and techniques. Yet, under the veneer of this freewheeling, fresh take on food lies the careful selection of ingredients that reflect Korea’s four distinct seasons. Meticulous rules dictate the perfect balance of subtle flavors.

properties.name: Sobajuu
properties.cuisine: B1 #22, 75 Keunumul-ro, Mapo-gu, Seoul, 04158, South Korea
properties.description: Sobajuu is a place that offers creative Japanese dishes using buckwheat as the main ingredient. It was initially run by two brothers, but now the younger sibling is in charge. By employing various cooking techniques, each course showcases different textures and flavors of buckwheat. For example, they offer self-made soba rolls with tuna and seaweed sauce, along with French-style buckwheat pancakes called galettes and grilled fish. The modern yet cozy space, combined with the clean presentation of the dishes, reflects the chef's dedication and attention to detail.

properties.name: Sobakeeri Suzu
properties.description: After honing his culinary skills in Japan, the owner-chef returned to reinterpret the beloved soba tradition using Korean buckwheat. Instead of following the typical buckwheat-to-flour ratios such as juwari or nihachi, the chef opted for sotoichi. The soba features a gentle grain aroma and a pleasantly chewy texture. You can enjoy classic zaru soba along with tasty braised dishes and crispy tempura, all great choices to pair with sake.

properties.name: Soigné
properties.cuisine: #201, 2F, Sinsa square, 652 Gangnam-daero, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06027, South Korea
properties.description: Soigné is celebrated for a tasting menu that reflects the changing seasons and brings each ingredient to life. Owner-chef Jun Lee blends Korean sensibilities with contemporary techniques, creating dishes articulated entirely in his own voice. His signature ‘Episode’ menus unfold like a classical narrative, from introduction to conclusion, as a paced culinary performance. In a minimalist, white-toned space centred on the kitchen, with elegant plating and attentive service, the dining experience is one to remember.

properties.name: Solbam
properties.description: An evening at Solbam begins with drinks and snacks in the atmospherically lit drawing room before you are led into a bright, spacious dining area with an open kitchen. Chef-owner Tae-jun Eom’s extensive experience and technical skill are put to excellent use, especially when paired with the best of Korea’s seasonal produce. Whether it is snow crab with cauliflower, abalone with bamboo shoots, or Hanwoo with dallae, the dishes are often original and striking, presented with a touch of theatricality by a highly formal service team. For the full experience, opt for the three- or seven-glass wine pairing.

properties.name: Sonnet
properties.description: Sonnet translates as 'a short song' in French, an apt name for a restaurant devoted to small plates. It offers a casual take on French cuisine, with a menu ranging from bite-sized morsels to desserts crafted using sophisticated culinary techniques. Cocoa rolls filled with chicken liver mousse, topped with truffle and raspberry jam, balance two distinct flavours, while the buckwheat pasta with fresh clams and caviar offers a subtle aromatic note.

properties.name: Sooksoodoga
properties.description: Sooksoodoga carefully selects the finest premium hanwoo beef, which is matured using proprietary methods for anywhere from 46 to 720 hours; the aim is to offer only the very best in aged meat.  The tasting menu offers diners a choice from three different cuts, accompanied by small Korean dishes. À la carte options are also available. The reasonable pricing, designed to make aged beef more accessible, is reason enough to visit.

properties.name: Joo052
properties.description: Joo052 is a portmanteau of 'Joo' (alcohol) and '052', the area code of the region the chefs live in. It is a Korean gastropub brimming with culinary ideas as playful as the restaurant’s name. Employing diverse fermented pastes and fermentation techniques, the basis of Korean cuisine, the chef duo offers unique dishes prepared with exceptional care and attention to detail to bring out unforgettable flavours. Additionally, the varied menu selections tend to go well together, making it easy to enjoy multiple items at once. These charms make Joo052 a perfect place to experience the exquisite harmony between Korean food and Korean alcohol.

properties.name: Soseoul Hannam
properties.cuisine: B1F, 21-18 Hannam-daero 20-gil, Yongsan-gu, Seoul, 04419, South Korea
properties.description: Inspired by local culinary traditions but dedicated to expressing the modern-day sensibilities of Seoul cuisine, Soseoul Hannam serves up contemporary Korean food using ingredients that are most familiar to the local palate. The chef's creations are an homage to some of the more typical dishes and preparation techniques, including seasoned salads, pan-fried and braised dishes. Kelp chips, rich soybean paste stew, sliced raw fish with gomchwi (Fischer's ragwort) and aged kimchi, and chargrilled webfoot octopus are some of its signature offerings. Embark on a flavorful and refined journey through Korea with this diverse collection of traditional liquors.

properties.name: Sosuheon
properties.description: Juxtaposed with Seoul’s contemporary skyline, a traditional hanok is home to Chef  Kyung-jae Park’s Sosuheon. The serene setting offers an elegant retreat from the city. From the eight-seat counter, diners can watch the confident chef at work as he prepares nigiri with quiet concentration. Prior to the nigiri course, a range of well-executed dishes is served, from cod roe with hairy crab to steamed tilefish and grilled hairtail. Highlights include gizzard shad and creamy cuttlefish, and a bowl of matcha brings the memorable evening to a close.

properties.name: Soul
properties.cuisine: B1F, 35 Sinheung-ro 26-gil, Yongsan-gu, Seoul, 04337, South Korea
properties.description: Located in a quaint part of Seoul, this operation is Chef Dae-hyun Yoon’s bold experiment in stylish fine dining. Here, the diverse culinary traditions and ingredients one may encounter in modern-day Korea converge with the familiar tastes found in everyday life to form unique fusion creations. Among the many connotations of the establishment’s name is the chef’s desire to serve food with soul, and this is palpable in every dish. Along with his wife, Chef Hee-eun Kim, this husband-and-wife duo have partitioned their establishment into several distinct sections, creating suitable dining spaces for disparate cuisines.

properties.name: STAY
properties.cuisine: 81F Lotteworld Tower, 300 Olympic-ro, Songpa-gu, Seoul, 05551, South Korea
properties.description: Located on the 81st floor, STAY offers a modern, luxurious dining experience in an elegant and comfortable setting. The menu, rooted in French technique, remains restrained rather than ornate, balancing meticulous detail with approachability. Some dishes are finished tableside, creating a natural sense of interaction, and the service is notably warm and attentive. The meal concludes with a delightful ritual at the central French Pastry Library, where guests may choose their preferred petits fours. A daytime visit is especially recommended—the expansive windows provide sweeping, refreshing views of the cityscape.

properties.name: Subaru
properties.description: Chef Young-chul Kang has spent years studying and refining the craft of soba making, driven by a single aim: to serve soba that matches the quality found in its birthplace. At Subaru, the soba follows the classic nihachi style and is made fresh using traditional Edo-period methods. Signature dishes include zaru soba, prized for its clean, pure flavour; soba with umami-rich duck and green onion; and soba with perilla oil. Diners can also enjoy foot-kneaded udon noodles, made in-house for a satisfyingly chewy texture.

properties.name: Sushi Matsumoto
properties.description: Chef Matsumoto Mizuho is renowned for replicating authenticity in local settings. Rather than chasing after gastronomic trends, this sushiya strives to faithfully reproduce the authentic sushi of mainland Japan amidst the Korean milieu. Such efforts are unmistakable—from the composition and arrangement of shari (rice) and neta (fish topping) to the knowledgeable service provided. The impressive line of tsumami (appetizers) and the progression of nigiri sushi —reflecting the natural cadence of flavors — remain the main culinary appeal.

properties.name: Table for Four
properties.description: This 'Table' uses a wide range of seasonal ingredients infused with optimal flavors from Taean, South Chungcheong Province—the chef’s hometown. His affection for the region and genuine passion to serve the freshest and healthiest food to patrons are the reasons behind his use of such choice. The chef is able to skillfully transform his hometown ingredients into exciting dishes, and at other times there is minimal culinary intervention. Diners who visit are bound to relish the allure of this European cuisine, imbued with the flair of Taean and accompanied by a wine list strong on Burgundy.

properties.name: Tasty Cube
properties.description: It is specialized in noodle dishes, with the signature being the hongtang and baektang noodles made from beef bone and meat. Another popular choice is kkaebusumyeon, a soupless tantanmen served with nutty zmajing sauce made from ground peanuts and sesame seeds. It offers Choyuringi, a chicken dish with a vinegar sauce, in small portions to accompany the noodles. Extra noodles are available free of charge, and the counter seating is ideal for solo diners.

properties.name: Tenjimon
properties.description: A collaboration between Chef Kim Dae-chun—owner of TOC TOC—and Chef Zaiyu Hasegawa—owner of Den in Tokyo—"Tenjimon' is the Japanese pronunciation of a Chinese/Korean word that means “To open the door to the gastronomic world via ingredients from heaven and earth.” Although this partnership requires that the menu be confirmed from Japan, Tenjimon’s omakase features fresh Korean ingredients. Of particular note, the signature hot pot rice is a uniquely flavored delicacy made with seasonal and local ingredients, such as amur wallflower and aster leaf. Since seating is limited for this unique culinary discovery, reservations are a must.

properties.name: Teppan
properties.address: B1F, Grand Hyatt Hotel, 322 Sowol-ro, Yongsan-gu, Seoul
properties.cuisine: B1F, Grand Hyatt Hotel, 322 Sowol-ro, Yongsan-gu, Seoul, 04347, South Korea
properties.description: Located along Grand Hyatt Seoul’s gourmet alley 322 Sowol-ro, Teppan is a restaurant where everything on the menu is cooked on an iron griddle — even appetizers and desserts. Food is prepared by chefs in front of the guests on flat-surface grills behind the bar counter. Diners can also gaze out at the scenic view of Namsan Mountain through the restaurant’s wide windows while enjoying their meal. The menu offers plenty of fresh seafood and meats as well as spectacular fire shows.
properties.address_ko: 용산구 소월로 322, 그랜드 하얏트 호텔 지하 1층, Seoul

properties.name: The Green Table
properties.cuisine: 5F Arario Space, 83 Yulgok-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul, 03058, South Korea
properties.description: Chef Kim Eun-hee applies French culinary precision to vegetables and grains, thoughtfully pairing them with traditional jangajji. Her risotto-style composition—built on barley, black rice, and oats—highlights root vegetables, bound together by a delicately expressive vegetable stock sauce, patiently simmered to reveal quiet depth. The ever-changing view of Changgyeonggung Palace lends a sense of place and serenity, while attentive yet unobtrusive service subtly elevates the overall dining experience.

properties.name: The Ninth Gate
properties.address: 1F The Westin Chosun Hotel, 106 Sogong-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul
properties.cuisine: 1F The Westin Chosun Hotel, 106 Sogong-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul, 04533, South Korea
properties.description: It would be challenging to discuss the evolution of French dining in Seoul without mentioning The Ninth Gate. Keeping up with Seoul’s ever-transforming restaurant scene, a newer and better The Ninth Gate reopened its doors to the delight of fans both old and new. While the food is still reflective of the classic French style from the days before its facelift, there is also greater sophistication that makes the restaurant more relevant than ever before. Wine-tasting programs are offered at the bar counter after 10 pm.
properties.address_ko: 중구 소공로 106, 웨스틴 조선 호텔 1층, Seoul

properties.name: Tim Ho Wan
properties.description: Tim Ho Wan has played a major role in making Hong Kong-style dim sum popular in many countries worldwide. The main force behind such growing popularity has been its policy of maintaining the traditional form of Hong Kong dim sum dishes and making them available at affordable prices. This management philosophy has been upheld in its branches in Seoul, making them as authentic as the original. The franchise’s capacious Samseong outpost, which is also the main location in Seoul, can serve up to 110 customers at one time and offers ample parking spaces. It is the perfect place to indulge in a wide variety of traditional dim sum.

properties.name: Toc Toc
properties.description: Chef Dae-chun Kim’s unwavering dedication to culinary excellence and his insistence on using only the finest ingredients are clearly reflected in the cuisine of Toc Toc. Chef Kim emphasises precision in technique to bring out the pure, natural flavours of each ingredient, highlighting his deep respect for the value of quality produce. His distinctive dishes are a testament to the care and creativity infused into every plate. In addition to his course menus, Toc Toc offers a thoughtfully curated selection of à la carte dishes, allowing guests to experience the full spectrum of flavours the restaurant has to offer.

properties.name: Toh Lim
properties.cuisine: 37F Lotte Hotel, 30 Eulji-ro, Jung-gu, Seoul, 04533, South Korea
properties.description: Located on the 37th floor of the Lotte Hotel, this Chinese restaurant offers a panoramic city view in a refined, East-inspired setting. The menu spans from dim sum to regional Chinese dishes, with a particular focus on seafood to accommodate a wide range of preferences. A signature highlight is the braised dish of matsutake mushrooms, abalone, and minced pork—a harmonious combination in which the mushroom’s subtle aroma and the abalone’s deep umami enrich the savory pork. Elegant private dining rooms of various sizes make it a fitting choice for family gatherings or business occasions.

properties.name: Tongue & Groove Joint
properties.description: Don’t expect to walk into a typical Korean barbecue restaurant when you visit Tongue and Groove Joint, a casually cool and modern space that feels more like a café or a beer hall. The restaurant specializes in quality Korean beef aged for at least three weeks as well as tender lamb chops and beef ribeye. These cuts are grilled at the table by the staff and come with a side of house-made chili oil and cilantro pesto which help cut the richness. The restaurant also has a good variety of lunch offerings and a wide selection of craft beers.

properties.name: Trid
properties.description: Diversity, deliciousness, and delight are the three D’s that form the basis for this restaurant's name. Trid focuses on these elements; in fact, it's the place where the perpetual culinary struggle to meet such standards is most strongly felt. The head chef has experienced diverse dining environments in different cultures, which has allowed him to make bold and interesting choices while pursuing new tastes and combinations of ingredients. The spatial cosiness, refined service, and elegantly designed open kitchen match the restaurant’s fare, creating a harmonious dining experience.

properties.name: Tutoiement
properties.description: The name Tutoiement means 'to speak in a friendly, informal way', reflecting Chef Do-hyeon Kim’s desire to connect casually with diners through food. Interestingly, in contrast to this relaxed approach, the precisely cooked and sophisticated French cuisine engages the senses, placing the focus firmly on the food. The chef expresses the subtle flavours, textures, and nuances of each ingredient with care, plating dishes to highlight harmony between the components and their accompanying sauces. His cooking reflects a personal journey, guided by clarity of vision and a desire to create a dining experience that feels both thoughtful and memorable.

properties.name: Vault Steakhouse
properties.description: The dimly lit masculine décor and a comprehensive whiskey collection with nearly 400 bottles to choose from make Vault+82 one of the most sought-after whiskey bars in Seoul. Right above it is the American-style Vault Steakhouse where premium USDA-approved American beef free of antibiotics and hormones is dry-aged for 14 and 28 days. Available cuts include T-bone, L-bone, bone-in ribeye, filet mignon, New York strip and boneless ribeye.

properties.name: VINHO
properties.cuisine: #162 Wellstone Bldg, 38 Hakdong-ro 43-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06059, South Korea
properties.description: Beneath an industrial-style ceiling, a dark-toned counter wraps around the open kitchen, allowing guests to observe each dish taking shape up close. Bright, upbeat music and the team’s easygoing service create an atmosphere that feels both relaxed and refined. Drawing on experience from well-known kitchens, co-owner Chef Sung-bin Jeon presents a seasonal menu that blends Korean tradition, modern technique, and personal interpretation. Subtle, expressive flavours and a bold yet minimalist aesthetic define the course experience. A thoughtfully curated wine pairing is recommended for those seeking a deeper journey.

properties.name: Woo Lae Oak
properties.description: Woo Lae Oak is one of the best-known Pyongyang naengmyeon restaurants in Seoul, and its traditions have been upheld since its opening in 1946. The broth is what sets this spot apart from other competitors. It is made by boiling Hanwoo foreshank for hours and seasoned with nothing more than salt and soy sauce to create the clean savoury flavour that can only be tasted here. The bulgogi, another popular dish, pairs well with the cold noodles. The restaurant has maintained consistent flavours  and has exclusively used local ingredients through its many years of service, which makes it packed with diners around the clock.

properties.name: Wooga
properties.cuisine: B1F, 22 Dosan-daero 49-gil, Gangnam-gu, Seoul, 06019, South Korea
properties.description: Wooga was originally born in Hoengseong, the beef capital of Korea. The restaurant is known for preparing the choicest quality local beef in its unique style: dry aged and well done. Chef Heo Se-byeong has been experimenting tirelessly with beef under the slogan meat science to maximize the characteristics of this meat such as tenderness, juiciness and flavor. Don’t miss out on another perennial favorite: a stew made with soybean paste fermented over long stages by the chef’s own mom.

properties.name: y'east
properties.description: y'east is home to unlimited culinary possibilities, just as the name suggests. Chef Young-dong Cho delights diners with familiar dishes reimagined via his singular aesthetics. He channels his passion into creating novel flavours by artfully blending ingredients that are hard to come by in Korea. From the signature amuse bouche inspired by kaya toast to galbi stone, each dish embodies the balance between novelty and familiarity, providing out-of-the-box gastronomic pleasures while retaining the essence of the original flavour.

properties.name: Yakitori Kiyu
properties.description: The experienced chefs’ busy hands deftly grill the chicken as the enticing aroma of Korean tojongdak yakitori fills the entire space. Yakitori Kiyu's goal is to take the flavours of yakitori to the next level. The restaurant uses chickens naturally raised on various medicinal herbs to provide diners with healthy, high-quality cuts of chicken. The menu features unlaid chicken eggs, which can only be obtained when a laying hen is slaughtered, highlighting the freshness of the ingredients.

properties.name: Yakitori Mook
properties.description: The chef prepares fresh tojongdak each day, offering a variety of cuts grilled over charcoal to showcase the bird’s unique flavors through the art of yakitori. A well-balanced marinade enhances the nuttiness and savory flavors of the meat. The tsukune, served with a poached egg, is tender and juicy, making it one of the most popular items alongside the negima. Do note that omakase pricing varies depending on the time, and ordering alcohol is required.

properties.name: Yangyang Memil Makguksu
properties.description: The not-so-secret secret to the longevity and success of Yangyang Memil Makguksu is that its noodles are made with 100% buckwheat and that they are made to order every time. The proprietor of the restaurant began his business many years ago for one reason only - buckwheat noodles were his favorite food. Although the restaurant specializes in the Gangwon-do Province-style cold buckwheat noodles, it also makes a fine bowl of spicy noodles composed with homemade kimchi. The basic spread is simple, but it’s rustic food at its best, made with love.

properties.name: Yonggeumok
properties.description: Since 1932, Yonggeumok has been offering piping hot bowls of loach soup, a popular local dish believed to boost energy, especially during the summer months. This restaurant serves two different versions of the hearty and comforting soup: the chunkier Seoul style with whole loach, and the smoother Jeolla province style with ground loach. Everything on the menu as well as the complimentary side dishes are prepared daily by the longtime owner and chef Han Jeong-ja.

properties.name: Youhan
properties.description: Yuhan is a small, chic Thai bistro. It can be difficult to find for first-time visitors, as it is hidden deep in the alleys of Hannam-dong. Established by Chef Kim Yu-han, who attended culinary school in Thailand, the restaurant offers familiar dishes with a creative twist. Diners can experience diverse flavors, including Thai sausages, seasonal sashimi, and dishes topped with various Thai curries . The restaurant also offers natural wine recommendations that perfectly pair with the dishes.

properties.name: Yu Yuan
properties.address: 11F Four Seasons Hotel, 97 Saemunan-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul
properties.cuisine: 11F Four Seasons Hotel, 97 Saemunan-ro, Jongno-gu, Seoul, 03183, South Korea
properties.description: On your way to your table, you’ll walk past a cabinet of Peking duck that’s a favorite order of many of the guests here at this handsome and comfortable Chinese restaurant at the Four Seasons hotel. While the menu leans towards Cantonese cuisine–lunchtime dim sum is always popular and the choice is extensive–the kitchen also offers specialities from a number of Chinese regions. Wok-fried Hoengseong Hanwood beef with ginger is a dish to look out for.

properties.name: Yukjeon Hoekwan
properties.description: Three generations spanning five decades have kept this restaurant in business since its opening in 1962. At the time, their iconic crispy thin-sliced bulgogi, grilled over open flames, was all that was offered on the menu. The dish became so popular that the name bassak bulgogi was patented. Today, the restaurant stands four stories high, still serving the same dish that has fed generations. Their coagulated beef blood soup with a clear broth is another stunner.

properties.name: YUN
properties.description: Chef-Owner Kim Do-yun is someone clearly passionate about ingredients, and he holds a particular fascination for noodles. The counter allows those lucky enough to have secured reservations a front-row seat to witness the dishes’ creation and hear the detailed explanations of the aging, fermenting and drying techniques that are used to add such depth of flavor and contrasting textures to the fish and meats that make up the multi-course set menu. This is cooking from a chef who never stops learning or developing his skills.

properties.name: Yurimmyeon
properties.description: For over 50 years, this family-run eatery has been serving bowls of noodles to those seeking comforting nourishment. The fresh buckwheat noodles, one of its bestsellers, are made from scratch, daily, using premium salt from Bigeumdo Island in Sinan County, and buckwheat flour from Bongpyeong. Topped with seasoned vegetables, thin strips of egg garnish, and a dollop of sweet and spicy sauce, a bowl of these noodles makes for a satisfying meal. Garak-guksu, wheat noodles in piping hot broth, comes with a generous serving of fishcake.

properties.name: Zero Complex
properties.description: Zero Complex has consistently presented innovative interpretations of the ingredients since its opening. Despite relocating twice, the flavours and creativity of the dishes have never wavered. The restaurant showcases the delightfully bouncy texture and rich, smoky flavour of sea mustard through one of their signature dishes—Pacific geoduck served with sides of chewy barley, sea spaghetti, and sea mustard mouse. Although the restaurant, located in a quiet residential area, might be a challenge to find, tasting the dishes while admiring the quiet garden will make it worth your while.

properties.name: Zhonghuafuchun Salon
properties.description: Zhonghuafuchun Salon is a fascinating establishment where traditional Chinese recipes, combined with local twists, translate into a richly flavourful feast. A cuisine that transforms and responds to changing times must be founded on the chef's clear and accurate understanding of the culinary tradition. Chef Bok-chun Nam’s dedication to reforming Chinese cuisine is unmistakable in his culinary creations. To promote a gradual evolution in his native cuisine, he pays particular attention to selecting quality ingredients while engaging in active exchanges with chefs in Mainland China.

properties.name: 영춘옥
properties.description:
Step into Yeongchunok (영춘옥), a beloved institution in Jongno that's been serving up hearty Korean comfort food for 85 years. This no-nonsense eatery, with its distinct retro vibe, is a popular spot for both locals and younger crowds drawn to its authentic atmosphere. Open 24 hours, it's the perfect place to grab a satisfying meal any time of day or night.

The star of the show is the rich Gomtang (곰탕), a clear, savory beef bone broth, and the Oxtail Gomtang (꼬리곰탕). The Oxtail Gomtang features generous, long-cut pieces of tender oxtail, served with a pile of fresh green onions that are best enjoyed dipped in the accompanying seasoned soy sauce. It's a simple yet deeply flavorful experience that embodies the essence of traditional Korean cuisine.

properties.name: 서북면옥
properties.description: Step into Seobuk Myeonok (서북면옥) for a taste of authentic Pyeongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). This beloved spot, with its nostalgic, old-school charm, is known for its deeply flavorful meat broth that perfectly complements the chewy buckwheat noodles. Don't miss their signature mul-naengmyeon (물냉면), a refreshing bowl that's surprisingly addictive and pairs wonderfully with a shot of soju (소주) - though be aware, soju isn't served on weekends and holidays. The handmade dumplings (만두) and tender suyuk (수육) are also highly recommended additions to your meal.

properties.name: 하동관
properties.description: This establishment is a hidden gem, a testament to dedication and quality that builds over time, much like renowned Seoul institutions. They focus on a clean, pure broth, reminiscent of the famous Haidonggwan (하동관) style, but with a unique twist – it's like Haidonggwan's gomtang (곰탕) without the tripe. Using only premium Korean beef, their clear, unadulterated soup is a hit with all ages, offering a healthy and comforting meal that stands apart from heavily seasoned alternatives. It's a place where the quality of ingredients shines through, requiring no artificial enhancements.

properties.name: 경인미술관전통다원
properties.description: Step into a tranquil oasis in the heart of Insadong at Gyeongin Traditional Tea House (경인미술관전통다원). This charming spot, formerly known as Gyeongin Dadang, offers a serene escape reminiscent of college days. Enjoy traditional teas, particularly the refreshing nokcha (녹차), served with a large mahot병 (mahot병) and a unique ritual of savoring the tea's color, aroma, and taste over three distinct experiences. The ambiance, with its traditional papered windows and the gentle glow of the moon, provides a perfect setting for quiet reflection and conversation.

properties.name: MAYBELL BAKERY
properties.description: Step into Maybell Bakery, a charming red brick establishment that feels like a hidden gem. Despite its popularity, with crowds often filling the space, the staff are incredibly accommodating, even managing to squeeze in extra seating for a group. The bakery offers a delightful array of healthy breads and coffee, making it a perfect spot for a relaxed catch-up with friends. Don't miss their signature coffee bread and fig rye bread (무화과호밀빵), paired with a cup of their subtly acidic, high-quality coffee.

properties.name: Onion
properties.description:
Step into Onion Anguk, a cafe that beautifully blends traditional Korean architecture with a modern, spacious vibe. Housed in a charming hanok (한옥), this spot offers a unique atmosphere with both traditional floor seating and contemporary table arrangements. It's a popular destination, especially for international visitors looking to experience a slice of Korean heritage, making it a bustling yet serene escape. The expansive pebble courtyard and the quaint rubber shoe decor add to its distinctive charm.

While the drinks, like the signature chocolate latte with a unique aroma, are noteworthy, the true stars are the pastries. With an overwhelming variety of freshly baked breads that look as good as they taste, choosing your treat is the hardest part. Pair your selection with a coffee or tea for a delightful break in the heart of Seoul.

properties.name: NAMUSAIRO
properties.description: Nestled in a charming hanok (한옥) style building, Namusairo offers a serene escape with its traditional architecture and hidden courtyards. This highly-awarded cafe, recognized for over a decade with Blue Ribbons, is a must-visit for coffee aficionados. While the basic iced americano (아이스 아메리카노) is already a crowd-pleaser with its rich, satisfying flavor, the real draw is their expertly roasted drip coffee, promising a complex and memorable cup. Don't miss out on their delicious desserts, with the tiramisu being a popular choice among patrons.

properties.name: 평양면옥
properties.description: A true taste of Pyongyang, Jangchung-dong Pyeongyang Naengmyeon has been serving its signature subtle broth and chewy buckwheat noodles since 1985. This Michelin-recognized establishment, now in its third generation, offers a refined experience. The star is the Pyeongyang naengmyeon (평양냉면) itself, a delicate yet deeply flavorful dish. Complement your noodles with their handmade mandu (만두), a half-portion of which is available, featuring a soft, tofu-rich filling with a satisfying crunch from bean sprouts.

properties.name: 우래옥
properties.description: Steeped in history since 1946, Woo Lae Oak is a revered institution for Pyongyang-style naengmyeon (평양냉면). Despite its long legacy, expect a significant wait, even on weekdays, as this spot draws crowds eager for its famously clean and delicate broth. The atmosphere is traditional and tidy, offering a comfortable space to enjoy a classic meal. Don't miss the opportunity to try their signature naengmyeon or take home their hearty yukgaejang (육개장).

properties.name: 평래옥
properties.description: Step into Pyeongraeok (평래옥), a venerable establishment in Euljiro (을지로) serving North Korean (이북) cuisine since 1950. While the signature Pyeongyang naengmyeon (평양냉면) offers a subtly savory chicken broth that might surprise even skeptics, the true stars for many are the handmade Pyeongyang mandu (평양 만두). These dumplings boast a delicate wrapper and a flavorful filling of meat and vegetables, making them a must-try. Don't miss the dak-muchim (닭무침) either – a refreshing, tangy, and slightly spicy chicken salad that perfectly complements the meal.

properties.name: 청진옥
properties.description: A true Seoul institution, Cheongjinok (청진옥) is a legendary Haejangguk (해장국) spot with nearly 90 years of history, recognized as a Seoul Future Heritage. This bustling eatery, often the first place locals think of for a restorative broth in Gwanghwamun, offers a comforting and clean soup experience. The atmosphere buzzes with a mix of solo diners and groups, reflecting its enduring popularity among locals and celebrities alike.

properties.name: 대성집
properties.description: Daesungjip is a legendary spot near Seongsu Station, famous for its signature dish, Beoseot Jumulleok (버섯주물럭). This unique stir-fry features tender, marinated chicken thigh from Chuncheon, combined with generous amounts of oyster mushrooms and a special sauce made with sun-dried chili peppers from Chochong. Unlike typical dakgalbi, the sauce is rich and deeply flavorful, with no added water, creating a sticky, addictive taste. The generous portions and the accompanying Pajeon (파전) and Muk-sabal (묵사발) make it a fantastic value, perfect for both lunch and lively dinners.

properties.name: The Ninth Gate Grille
properties.description: Nestled within The Westin Chosun Seoul, The Ninth Gate Grille offers a contemporary Western dining experience with a view of the historic Hwangudan Altar. While it may have started as Korea's first French restaurant, it now presents an international brasserie style. The Chef's Tasting Dinner showcases a range of dishes, from a delightful parmesan cheese amuse-bouche and fresh shrimp with asparagus to a standout miso-glazed black cod that blends Japanese and Western flavors. The Korean beef tenderloin is tender and perfectly cooked, complemented by a selection of fine salts. The meal concludes with a refreshing herbal lemonade and a light grapefruit mousse.

properties.name: 무수옥
properties.description: Step into Musuok (무수옥), a venerable institution that has been serving its signature seolleongtang (설렁탕) since 1943. This no-frills, generations-old eatery offers a comforting and deeply satisfying experience, perfect for those seeking authentic Korean flavors. The broth is rich and nuanced, and the accompanying kimchi (김치) and kkakdugi (깍두기) are house-made and perfectly complement the main dish. Don't miss the chance to try their yukhoe bibimbap (육회비빔밥) and fresh yukhoe (육회) as well, which have quickly become favorites among diners.

properties.name: Napoleon
properties.description: Napoleon is a charming cafe conveniently located right next to Wonju Station, making it the perfect spot for travelers needing a quick break or a place to wait for their train. The atmosphere is welcoming, with a friendly owner and a spotlessly clean drink preparation area. Prices are remarkably affordable, with Americanos available in the 2,000 KRW range, a rarity in independent cafes today. They offer a variety of freshly baked goods, from simple morning buns to sweet red bean bread (danpatppang), all at budget-friendly prices.

properties.name: Richemont bakery
properties.description: Richemont bakery

properties.name: 이태리재
properties.description: Nestled in the charming alleys of Bukchon, Italyjae (이태리재) offers a unique Italian dining experience within a beautifully renovated Hanok (한옥). The restaurant, previously recognized by the Michelin Guide, specializes in cicchetti (치케티), a Venetian-style small bites concept. Expect a cozy atmosphere, attentive service, and a delightful array of meticulously prepared small dishes, perfect for sharing.

properties.name: 두가헌
properties.description: Nestled within a traditional Hanok (한옥) setting, this venue offers a unique and romantic atmosphere, especially when viewed during a pre-ceremony preview. The staff are noted for their exceptional kindness, contributing to a pleasant and memorable visit. While the ceremony space itself might feel a bit intimate, the potential for stunning floral arrangements can transform it into a truly magical setting for any special occasion.

properties.name: 토속촌
properties.description: Nestled near Gyeongbok Palace and Cheong Wa Dae, Tosokchon Samgyetang is a beloved institution famous for its nourishing chicken soup. Be prepared for a potential wait, especially during peak hours, as this spot draws crowds eager for a taste of their signature dish. The restaurant offers a traditional ambiance, and while parking is available, it's limited, so considering nearby public parking is a good idea. They also offer takeout for those who want to enjoy their meal elsewhere.

properties.name: 키즈나
properties.description: Nestled in a traditional Hanok-style building with a serene garden, Kizuna offers an intimate and private dining experience. Chef Song Woong-sik, one of Seoul's "Four Great Sushi Chefs," leads the counter, providing a tranquil atmosphere perfect for special occasions. While the chef is known for his quiet demeanor, the focus is on the exquisite sushi, with guests encouraged to ask for explanations if desired.

properties.name: 몽탄
properties.description: Step into Mongtan (몽탄), a highly sought-after barbecue joint in Yongsan, often hailed as one of Seoul's top three meat destinations. While famous for its notoriously long waits, the reward is worth it, especially for their signature straw-fired beef ribs (우대갈비). The restaurant boasts a traditional, multi-story building with a lively first floor where the grilling action happens and a more serene second floor for dining. Prepare for a culinary experience that lives up to the hype, with an array of delicious banchan (밑반찬) and sauces to complement your meal.

properties.name: 금돼지식당
properties.description:
Prepare for a popular pilgrimage to Geumdwaeji Sikdang (금돼지식당), a celebrity-frequented spot that boasts Michelin and Blue Ribbon accolades. Be ready for a significant wait, as even arriving early doesn't guarantee immediate seating. The vibe is buzzing, with patrons eager to sample the highly-touted pork dishes. 

The signature dishes include the rich Bon Samgyeopsal (본삼겹) and the tender Nun-kkot Moksal (눈꽃목살), both praised for their quality. Don't miss the unique Basil Ssam (바질쌈) for a refreshing twist, and the hearty Dongdaeji Kimchi Jjigae (통돼지 김치찌개) is the perfect accompaniment to the succulent pork.

properties.name: Panello
properties.description: Panello is a highly sought-after Italian restaurant in Hapjeong, famous for its authentic Neapolitan-style pizzas and pastas. It's a popular spot for dates and small gatherings, so reservations are essential, often needing to be made several days in advance. The intimate, dimly lit ambiance, coupled with a wide selection of wines, creates a sophisticated atmosphere perfect for a special evening out. Their commitment to tradition is evident, having received certification from the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN).

properties.name: Matthew
properties.description:
Matthew offers a chic and intimate French dining experience in the trendy Seongsu-dong neighborhood, perfect for special occasions like birthdays and anniversaries. While the exterior boasts a modern concrete facade, the interior is surprisingly cozy and casual, making it an ideal spot for dates and intimate gatherings. The menu features set courses for lunch and dinner, with options like delicate seafood appetizers, seasonal fish dishes, and creative pasta, all presented with French flair. 

The culinary journey at Matthew is a delightful exploration of refined flavors and textures. Start with the refreshing amuse-bouche, followed by exquisite appetizers such as the sweet shrimp tartare and a luxurious clam dish. The main courses, including a tender sea bream with clam chowder sauce and a creamy anolotti pasta, showcase seasonal ingredients. While the main protein dishes like quail and lamb rack might leave some wanting more, the experience is beautifully rounded off with exceptional desserts, including a delightful buckwheat ice cream and mille-feuille, leaving a sweet and lasting impression.

properties.name: JUEUN
properties.description: Chef Park Ju-eun's eponymous restaurant, JUEUN, offers a refined and elegant Korean dining experience. The tasting menu showcases a delicate balance of textures and flavors, from the buckwheat steamed bun (증편) with its crisp exterior and tender interior to the refreshing mulhoe (물회) featuring thinly sliced sea bass and a vibrant apple-infused gochujang sauce. Each dish is meticulously crafted, highlighting seasonal ingredients and Chef Park's innovative approach to traditional Korean cuisine.

properties.name: GiGAS
properties.description: Chef Jeong Hwan's Mediterranean restaurant, Gigas, offers a refined and innovative dining experience. The vegetarian tasting menu is a journey through artfully prepared dishes, highlighting seasonal ingredients like crisp radishes with creamy herb sauce and earthy beets complemented by passionfruit sabayon. Each course is a delicate balance of flavors and textures, showcasing the chef's meticulous attention to detail and creative use of produce.

properties.name: CESTA
properties.description: CESTA, meaning 'basket' in Spanish, offers a culinary experience focused on drawing out the natural flavors of fresh, seasonal ingredients through expert techniques, often utilizing charcoal. The ambiance is sophisticated yet welcoming, complemented by an impressive selection of wines and a chef's commitment to accessible fine dining. Signature dishes include the delicate Sea Bream Tartare (참돔 타르타르) with chrysanthemum oil and the rich Sweet Shrimp Tartare Mini Sandwich (단새우 타르타르 미니샌드) featuring XO sauce and sea tangle (감태).

properties.name: XESC MENZL
properties.description: This charming spot in Seongsu-dong is a haven for charcuterie (샤퀴테리) lovers, offering a cozy atmosphere perfect for wine gatherings. They boast an impressive selection of house-made cured meats and sausages, with the 'Bokki' (보끼), a mixed cold cut platter, being a highly recommended starting point to sample their diverse flavors. The restaurant also offers a fantastic perk: one free bottle of wine corkage, making it ideal for bringing your own favorite bottle to pair with their delicious offerings. Don't miss their surprisingly excellent house-smoked salmon, praised for its firm texture and lack of fishiness, or the unique pickled artichoke side dish.

properties.name: 오장동흥남집
properties.description: Step into a Seoul institution renowned for its unique take on naengmyeon (냉면). This spot offers a minimalist dining experience, with warm broth served as the only accompaniment to your meal. The star of the show is the bibim naengmyeon (비빔냉면), a dish where the flavorful meat and other toppings are cleverly hidden beneath the noodles, offering a delightful surprise with every bite. It's a place that encourages you to customize your experience, making each bowl a personal culinary adventure.

properties.name: 오장동함흥냉면
properties.description: Step into a piece of Seoul's culinary history at Ojang-dong Heungnamjip, a legendary establishment serving Hamheung-style naengmyeon (함흥냉면) since 1953. This beloved spot is a cornerstone of the Ojang-dong Naengmyeon Street, a district once bustling with noodle shops. The star of the show is their signature Hamheung naengmyeon, known for its chewy, thin noodles. Diners rave about the Hoe Naengmyeon (회냉면 - raw fish naengmyeon) and the Gogi Naengmyeon (고기냉면 - meat naengmyeon), with many opting for the "seokkeo" (섞어 - mixed) version, combining both delicious toppings. Don't miss the incredibly flavorful broth served before your meal – it's so good, you'll want a second cup!

properties.name: default value
properties.description: Step into a haven for coffee aficionados, helmed by a national barista champion. This chic, white-toned cafe offers a serene atmosphere perfect for both focused work and relaxed enjoyment. Their signature latte, 'Thank You Berry Much,' is a revelation, boasting a rich, creamy texture with delightful strawberry and cheesecake notes that reviewers rave about as a 'life-best.' For a truly transcendent coffee experience, their siphon-brewed Geisha coffee is a must-try, offering a complex flavor profile with notes of raspberry, cherry, and tropical fruits.

properties.name: Mesh Coffee
properties.description: Mesh Coffee, a renowned coffee spot from Seoul, has landed in Jeju, bringing its signature minimalist and cozy vibe to the island. The interior boasts a clean, wooden aesthetic with ample seating and large windows perfect for a relaxing coffee break. It's the ideal spot for a morning pick-me-up, conveniently located across from the famous D&D photo zone in Tap-dong.

properties.name: EL Cafe
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet residential alley of Huam-dong, EL CAFE Roasters is a hidden gem known only to those in the know. This charming cafe, recognized for its 13 consecutive Blue Ribbon awards, offers a cozy and clean interior with an open layout. While seating is limited, it's the perfect spot for a post-meal coffee or a meeting. The cafe also doubles as a roastery, with a dedicated space showcasing their coffee-making machinery and a wide array of their own roasted beans, coffee capsules, drip bags, and brewing equipment available for purchase. Don't miss the complimentary daily tasting coffee to sample their signature roasts.

properties.name: Coffee Libre
properties.description: Coffee Libre Yeonnam is a beloved spot, holding the distinction of being the brand's very first location. Housed in a charming, renovated traditional Korean house, this cafe has long been celebrated for its commitment to in-house roasting, allowing coffee aficionados to truly savor the bean's inherent flavors. The atmosphere is cozy, with the iconic blue mask mascot adding a touch of personality. They offer a variety of expertly brewed coffees, including a notable decaf option, perfect for those seeking a flavorful yet caffeine-free experience.

properties.name: IDENTITY COFFEE LAB
properties.description: Step into Identity Coffee Lab, a bright and airy space in Hongdae, perfect for enjoying a sunny afternoon. While they offer a variety of coffee options, their filter coffee, especially the Honduras Geisha (온두라스 게이샤), is a standout, praised for its clean taste and subtle acidity. The cafe also serves biscotti and cookies, reminiscent of their previous location, and offers oat milk as a dairy alternative for their lattes and cappuccinos.

properties.name: Deep blue lake
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet alley of Mangwon-dong, Deep Blue Lake is a renowned roastery cafe that prioritizes quality with a minimalist, clean aesthetic. They exclusively use beans with a Cup of Excellence (C.o.E.) score of 86 or higher, employing precise roasting techniques. Their signature offerings include the 'Deep Blending,' a rich mix of Indian, Colombian, and Ethiopian beans with a dark chocolate finish, and the 'Blue Blending,' a brighter combination of Ethiopian natural and Honduran beans with fruity notes.

properties.name: 휴135
properties.description: Nestled in Hannam-dong, Hyu135 offers a sophisticated dining experience with both open seating and private rooms, perfect for intimate gatherings. The restaurant is renowned for its dry-aged steaks, with a visible aging process displayed, and its expertly crafted 솥밥 (sotbap - rice cooked in a pot). While the Christmas special offered a trio of dry-aged cuts (안심 - tenderloin, 채끝 - sirloin, 등심 - ribeye), the menu also features exquisite seafood starters like the 신안 1004 굴 슈터 (Shinan 1004 oyster shooter) with tobiko and quail egg yolk, and a refreshing 단새우 세비체 (sweet shrimp ceviche) optimized for local palates. The ambiance is refined, making it an ideal spot for special occasions and those who appreciate a well-curated course meal.

properties.name: Butcheli House
properties.description: Nestled in the trendy Hannam-dong, Butcheli House is a steakhouse that delivers a memorable dining experience. While some dishes might miss the mark, the star of the show is undoubtedly their steaks. The signature naedeungsim (내등심) is a must-try, offering a delightful interplay of textures and flavors that truly showcases the chef's confidence. The pan con tomate (빵 con tomate) is a simple yet satisfying starter, with perfectly toasted bread and a fresh tomato topping.

properties.name: BLT Steak
properties.description: Nestled within the JW Marriott Dongdaemun Square Seoul, BLT Steak offers a sophisticated dining experience perfect for special occasions. The open kitchen provides a tantalizing aroma of grilled meats upon entry, and the attentive service, including warm hand towels and helpful menu recommendations, enhances the overall ambiance. While the bread was noted as slightly less impressive than in the past, the meal progresses with elegant Villeroy & Boch plates and a delightful amuse-bouche.

properties.name: 댓잎갈비
properties.description: Step into a culinary time capsule at Daetnip Galbi (댓잎갈비), a beloved pork rib joint that has been serving since 1987. The star of the show is their signature galbi (갈비), marinated for 100 hours in a unique blend of bamboo sap, persimmon leaves, plantain, and kudzu leaves, then grilled over charcoal. This special preparation results in incredibly tender, melt-in-your-mouth pork ribs that are a must-try. The restaurant, a converted house, offers a charming atmosphere with a small garden perfect for a post-meal coffee, and if you snag a seat by the window, you can enjoy views of the Gangbyeonbuk-ro highway and the Han River.

properties.name: 금룡
properties.description: Nestled within the Grand Walkerhill Seoul, Geumryong offers a modern take on Cantonese cuisine, aiming for the pinnacle of Chinese culinary art. The ambiance is refined, with private rooms available for a more intimate dining experience. Start your meal with a vibrant array of appetizers, featuring delights like seasoned jellyfish, abalone, and salmon. Don't miss the highly recommended Mango Shrimp (망고새우), a perfect marriage of crispy fried shrimp and sweet mango sauce. Another standout is the Snow Crab Meat Geumhwa-eochi Gratin (대게살 금화어치 구이), a rich and flavorful dish served in a crab shell, topped with succulent crab meat and cheese.

properties.name: FELL&COLE
properties.description: Nestled in a charming alley near Sangsu Station, FELL&COLE is a delightful ice cream haven with a vibrant blue exterior. The interior is a whimsical wonderland, adorned with adorable SNOOPY and CHARLIE BROWN figurines and merchandise, making it a fun spot to browse while you wait for your treat. They offer a tempting array of unique flavors, including a refreshing honeydew-melon sorbet (참외소르베) and a rich chocolate option, perfect for a sweet escape.

properties.name: 발우공양
properties.description: Nestled within a temple stay building in Insadong, Balwoo Gongyang offers a serene and private dining experience, with all seating in separate rooms perfect for intimate gatherings. This vegetarian temple food (sachal-eumsik, 사찰음식) restaurant provides a refined culinary journey, featuring meticulously prepared courses like the 'Won Sik' (원식). Highlights include a uniquely smooth and subtly sweet red bean porridge (patjuk, 팥죽) and an intriguing chewy, dried acorn jelly (malrin dotorimuk bokkeum, 말린 도토리묵볶음) that offers a delightful textural contrast.

properties.name: 모모야마
properties.description: Perched on the 38th floor of the Lotte Hotel Seoul, Momoyama offers a sophisticated omakase (오마카세) experience with breathtaking city views. This Michelin-selected establishment is renowned for its exquisite sushi and refined Japanese cuisine. The ambiance is perfect for special occasions, with counter seating allowing diners to witness the chefs' artistry firsthand. Freshly grated wasabi and meticulously prepared dishes, like the delicate steamed egg with crab and the tender braised abalone, set the stage for an unforgettable culinary journey.

properties.name: Tian mi mi
properties.description: Step into Tian mi mi (티엔미미), a chic Chinese restaurant helmed by the renowned "Queen of Dumplings," Chef Jeong Ji-seon, celebrated for her appearance on Netflix's 'Chef's Table'. The ambiance is sophisticated, fitting for its location within the Mercure Ambassador Hotel, and reservations are highly recommended due to its immense popularity. Prepare for a culinary journey where classic Chinese flavors are elevated with exquisite presentation and masterful technique.

properties.name: Deepin Ok-Su
properties.description: Deepin Ok-Su is the highly sought-after culinary venture of Chef Yoon Nam-no, a fan favorite from the "Black and White Chef" program. Securing a reservation here is a challenge, often requiring persistent effort through Catch Table's waitlist system. The restaurant boasts a unique, communal seating arrangement with long, linear tables, fostering a lively atmosphere where guests can share the dining experience. It's a place where diners are encouraged to explore the menu extensively, indulging in a variety of dishes.

properties.name: 도량
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Seochon neighborhood, just a two-minute walk from Gyeongbokgung Station, Doryang (도량) offers a sophisticated dining experience. The interior blends the feel of a high-end Chinese restaurant with antique touches, creating an elegant ambiance. Be prepared for a wait, as this popular spot, helmed by Chef Im Tae-hoon of "Heukbaek Yolisah" and "Cheolgabang Yolisah" fame, draws crowds eager to secure a table. Securing a reservation is highly recommended, but early morning walk-ins might get lucky with a bit of fortune.

properties.name: YAKITORI MOOK
properties.description: Yakitori Mook offers a unique tojongdak (토종닭) omakase experience, showcasing various cuts of native Korean chicken prepared with expert grilling. The intimate counter seating allows you to witness the chefs' meticulous preparation, while the menu features creative dishes like chicken liver with blueberry compote and perfectly grilled vegetables. Complement your meal with their recommended shiso highball (시소 하이볼) or sake, and don't miss the refreshing shiso ice cream (시소 아이스크림) for dessert.

properties.name: 진진
properties.description: This Michelin-recognized gem offers a vibrant and bustling atmosphere, perfect for gatherings with friends. While reservations are recommended, they can be surprisingly easy to secure. The menu boasts a variety of expertly prepared dishes, with the shrimp toast (멘보샤) standing out as a signature item, praised for its crispy exterior and juicy, flavorful filling. Other highlights include the rich crab meat stir-fry (대게살볶음) and the aromatic steamed rockfish (칭찡우럭) with its delightful medley of scallions, ginger, and cilantro.

properties.name: Ca' Del Lupo
properties.description: Nestled in a charming Hanok (한옥) in the heart of Hyojadong, Ca' Del Lupo offers a unique Italian dining experience steeped in traditional Korean architecture. The ambiance is cozy and intimate, reminiscent of a warm home, making it an ideal spot for deep conversations and relaxed gatherings. The restaurant boasts an impressive wine list, perfect for those looking to unwind and enjoy a sophisticated evening.

properties.name: SEOUL DINING
properties.description: Perched near the iconic N Seoul Tower, Bu-eong-i Donkatsu offers a dining experience with a breathtaking panoramic view of the city. This popular spot, recently featured on a travel show, provides a cozy and elegant atmosphere perfect for enjoying a meal while taking in the sights. Whether bathed in sunlight or illuminated by the evening glow, the restaurant's ambiance complements its stunning vistas, making it an ideal stop before or after exploring the tower and its surroundings.

properties.name: 카덴
properties.description: Nestled in Yeonhui-dong, Udon Kaden is the acclaimed establishment of celebrity chef Jeong Ho-yeong, known for his appearances on popular Korean cooking shows. The restaurant specializes in a wide variety of udon dishes, with the Bukkake Udon (붓카케우동) being a standout. Diners rave about the chewy, springy udon noodles and the perfectly crispy, yet tender, Chicken Karaage (치킨가라아게) that complements the noodles beautifully. It's a must-visit for udon enthusiasts seeking expertly crafted Japanese comfort food.

properties.name: 옥동식
properties.description: Prepare for a serious wait at Okdongsik (옥동식), a Michelin-recommended spot that draws crowds even before opening. Arrive early, as the queue forms well before the 11 AM start, with many customers securing their spot hours in advance. Despite the long wait, the anticipation is palpable for this highly sought-after dining experience.

properties.name: 계향각
properties.description: Step into Gyehyanggak (계향각), a sophisticated Chinese restaurant specializing in the unique cuisine of the Qing Dynasty, a rarity in Korea. Helmed by renowned Professor Shin Gye-sook, the ambiance is refined and intimate, making it an ideal spot for special occasions or family gatherings. Reservations are highly recommended due to its popularity and cozy setting, with private rooms available for a more secluded dining experience.

properties.name: 산청숯불가든
properties.description: Prepare for a significant wait at this popular spot, famous for its Jirisan black pork (지리산 흑돼지) grilled over traditional charcoal. The atmosphere is lively and bustling, filled with the energy of diners enjoying their meals, making it a great place for lively gatherings but perhaps not for quiet conversation. The rustic interior evokes a traditional countryside eatery, and the extensive banchan (밑반찬) spread offers a delightful variety of flavors to complement the star attraction.

properties.name: Cucciolo
properties.description: Nestled within the stunning Amorepacific headquarters, Cucciolo Seoul offers a chic Italian dining experience with a sophisticated ambiance. The restaurant boasts an impressive wine selection, with a dedicated wine shop where guests can explore and purchase bottles, making it a perfect spot for wine enthusiasts. With comfortable seating options, including a charming outdoor terrace, and a stylish interior featuring unique furniture, Cucciolo is ideal for everything from casual lunches and romantic dates to family gatherings and private events.

properties.name: Modern Shabu House
properties.description: Modern Shabu House offers a sophisticated yet accessible dining experience, perfect for those seeking an elevated take on a classic Korean favorite. The ambiance is chic and modern, making it an ideal spot for both casual lunches and special occasions. With a wide array of broth options, including unique choices like coconut green curry and kimchi rosé risotto, and the flexibility to change broths mid-meal, diners can embark on a personalized culinary journey. The unlimited meat and vegetable refills ensure a satisfying and abundant meal, highlighting the restaurant's commitment to generous portions and quality ingredients.

properties.name: 만리지화
properties.description: Manlijihwa is a highly-regarded eel restaurant located in Gwanghwamun D Tower, perfect for a date or a comfortable meal with friends and family. This establishment has earned consecutive Blue Ribbon Survey certifications from 2024 to 2026, a testament to its quality. The ambiance is relaxed, with spacious table settings and intimate lighting, creating a pleasant dining experience. They offer a variety of 'hansang' (한상) set menus, with their signature dish being the eel. The eel is prepared using premium, tender 3.5-mi (3.5미) eel, grilled over bijangtan charcoal for an exceptionally rich flavor. Don't miss their unique 'nuruk' (누룩) salt dressing on the fresh, individual salads.

properties.name: 남영돈
properties.description:
Prepare for a wait at Namyeongdon (남영돈), a highly-regarded pork barbecue joint in Seoul. The restaurant emphasizes its use of high-quality charcoal, which is openly displayed at the entrance. Inside, the atmosphere is lively with spacious tables, and the side dishes are plentiful and homemade, featuring kimchi and various pickled vegetables. The menu is focused on premium pork cuts, with options like the crispy pork jowl (hangjeongsal - 항정살), tender pork neck (gabrisal - 가브리살), and juicy pork belly (samgyeopsal - 삼겹살). 

The staff at Namyeongdon are attentive, grilling each cut of meat to perfection right at your table. They expertly cook the pork belly, which is noted for its five layers (ogyeopsal - 오겹살), achieving a delightful crispy exterior and juicy interior. The thick iron grill ensures even cooking, and the meat is served in bite-sized pieces, ready to be enjoyed with the array of banchan (반찬) or on its own. It's a place where the quality of the meat and the skillful preparation shine.

properties.name: 호반
properties.description: This is a prime opportunity to secure a new 34-pyeong apartment in Seoul for under 900 million KRW, significantly cheaper than comparable developments. The building offers a range of amenities including a fitness center, indoor golf practice range, and a library, all within a well-landscaped environment. Its strategic location near Oru-dong Station (오류동역) on Line 1 provides excellent connectivity to Seoul's central districts and major transportation hubs like Gwangmyeong Station (광명역), with future GTX-B line access enhancing its appeal. The surrounding area boasts convenient access to major roads like Gyeongin-ro (경인로) and Nambu Circular Road (남부순환로), as well as reputable public schools like Oryu Elementary School (오류 초등학교) and Gaebong Middle School (개봉중학교).

properties.name: 홍연
properties.description: Nestled within the historic Westin Chosun Seoul, Hong Yeon offers a refined yet family-friendly Chinese dining experience. The ambiance is sophisticated yet comfortable, making it an ideal spot for family outings, with attentive service that caters to even the youngest diners. Its prime location near City Hall provides easy access and the convenience of a post-meal stroll around the beautiful Hwan-gu Dan (환구단) grounds.

properties.name: Flavour town
properties.description:
Flavour Town offers a vibrant fusion Chinese dining experience in the trendy Seongsu-dong area. The atmosphere buzzes with a modern, chic vibe, making it a popular spot, especially on weekends. While parking isn't available on-site, its proximity to Seoul Forest Station makes it accessible. Be prepared for potential waits, as the cozy interior fills up quickly, or consider booking through Catchtable to secure your spot.

The star of the show is undoubtedly the crispy yet tender Jokbal Twigim (족발튀김), a must-try that pairs perfectly with rice, earning its 'rice thief' reputation. Another highly recommended dish is the Shrimp Toast (새우 토스트), featuring layers of delicate cheong fun filled with plump shrimp, complemented by a savory peanut sauce and a hint of spice. The menu also boasts other delights like Chongqing Chicken and Mapo Tofu, offering a unique culinary adventure.

properties.name: Piece
properties.description: Piece, a Chinese restaurant located in Gourmet 494 in Hannam-dong, boasts a long history dating back to its founding in Cheongdam-dong in 2013.
