With a bright, airy interior and a coveted terrace, it's the perfect spot to relax. The star of the show is the selection of over 20 types of bread, primarily natural sourdoughs like ciabatta (치아바타) and baguettes, crafted by a renowned patissier focusing on taste and health.

properties.name: Churi Churi
properties.description: Step into Sojindam, a charming dessert cafe in Garosu-gil that blends traditional Korean aesthetics with modern sweet creations. The exterior might resemble a traditional rice cake shop, but inside, a delightful array of cakes awaits, many infused with classic Korean flavors. From the earthy sweetness of mugwort and red bean in the 'Patisuksook' (팥이쑥쑥) cake to the rich 'Heukim Injeolmi' (흑임자인절미) and the decadent Red Velvet, there's a unique cake for every palate. The interior offers a cozy hanok (한옥) vibe with both table seating and traditional floor seating options, making it a perfect spot to relax and indulge.

properties.name: 최사장네닭
properties.description: Step into Choi Sajangne Dak (최사장네닭) near Hongik University Station for a taste of authentic, comforting Korean cuisine. This long-standing eatery, marked by a nostalgic sign and walls adorned with celebrity autographs, offers a cozy and surprisingly quiet escape from the bustling Hongdae area. It's a perfect spot for a hearty meal, whether you're dining solo or with company, with a welcoming atmosphere that feels like a familiar favorite.

properties.name: 청송함흥냉면
properties.description: This Yeonnam-dong spot is a go-to for refreshing cold noodles, especially on a warm day. While they offer other dishes like galbijjim (갈비찜) and bulgogi (불고기) during the colder months, their specialty is the Hamheung-style naengmyeon (함흥냉면). Don't miss the bibim naengmyeon (비빔냉면) or the unique hoe naengmyeon (회냉면) topped with fermented skate. Pair your noodles with their handmade mandu (만두) for a satisfying meal.

properties.name: 철철복집
properties.description: Step into Cheolcheol Bokjip (철철복집), a renowned pufferfish specialist in the bustling Euljiro area. This spot is famous for its visually striking pufferfish dishes, particularly the grilled pufferfish (bok-sogeum-gui, 복소금구이), which is prepared tableside. While the intimate, retro setting can feel a bit cramped and the prices are steep, the unique experience and the delicate, clean flavor of the pufferfish make it a memorable destination for those seeking a distinctive Korean culinary adventure.

properties.name: 진아춘
properties.description: Step into Jinachun (진아춘), a historic Chinese restaurant in Daehangno (대학로) that has been serving customers since 1925. This establishment, recognized as a Seoul Future Heritage site, offers a nostalgic ambiance with its traditional Chinese decor and a variety of Chinese liquors. While its exact claim to being Seoul's oldest Chinese restaurant is debated, its status as a long-standing institution is undeniable, making it a unique culinary destination.

properties.name: 참숯골
properties.description: This newly opened spot near Yongsan Station is a prime destination for high-quality Korean BBQ, especially for corporate gatherings. The restaurant boasts a spacious and clean interior, capable of hosting up to 50 people, making it ideal for year-end parties and company dinners. They specialize in premium Hanwoo (한우) beef, with cuts like sirloin (등심), *jebichuri* (제비추리), and *tosal* (토시살) aging beautifully in a visible display. The attentive service, where staff grill the meat to perfection, allows for a truly relaxed dining experience.

properties.name: 진어참치
properties.description: A 14-time consecutive Blue Ribbon Survey selection, Jin-eo Tuna (진어참치) is a top-tier tuna destination in Seoul, consistently delivering a satisfying experience. The restaurant offers a refined atmosphere with counter seats, private rooms, and regular tables, making it suitable for both intimate dinners and group gatherings. They pride themselves on serving impeccably prepared bluefin tuna (참다랑어), so much so that they forgo traditional accompaniments like soy sauce or seaweed, letting the pure flavor of the fish shine.

properties.name: Coffeest
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet neighborhood near Gyeongbok Palace, Coffeest offers a serene escape from the bustling city. This historic cafe, operating since 2003, boasts a cozy interior filled with books, creating a perfect ambiance for quiet contemplation or hushed conversations. Its unique location, built partially on the historic Gyeonghuigung Palace site, adds a touch of historical intrigue, especially with the visible preserved ruins beneath the restroom pathway. The cafe is a beloved spot for those seeking a peaceful retreat, often frequented by visitors from the nearby Seoul Museum of Art.

properties.name: ITER MYEONGDONG
properties.description: Step into ITER MYEONGDONG and discover a hidden gem in the heart of Seoul. This restaurant offers a sophisticated yet cozy ambiance, perfect for intimate conversations and special occasions. The minimalist interior, with its clean white and cream tones complemented by warm lighting, creates a serene escape from the bustling city. With ample space between tables and meticulous attention to detail in everything from the tableware to the menu curation, ITER MYEONGDONG provides a dining experience where you feel truly valued and pampered.

properties.name: 제제
properties.description: Step into JEJE (제제), a modern Chinese restaurant helmed by a chef from the renowned Palais de Chine. The ambiance is chic and contemporary, with thoughtful touches like coat racks and a comfortable waiting area. JEJE offers a sophisticated dining experience, perfect for both intimate meals and private group gatherings in their separate rooms. Reservations are highly recommended.

properties.name: Joo052
properties.description: Joo052 offers a harmonious blend of Korean cuisine and traditional liquors, reflecting the chef's deep understanding of their synergy. The name itself, combining 'Joo' (酒, alcohol) with the chef's hometown area code, hints at this focus. They pride themselves on using fresh ingredients sourced nationwide and crafting essential bases like doenjang (된장, soybean paste), ganjang (간장, soy sauce), and sikcho (식초, vinegar) in-house. Even familiar dishes like ssam (쌈, wraps) and bibim guksu (비빔국수, spicy mixed noodles) are elevated with a subtle, yet noticeable, difference. For a special pairing, consider their 'Gaia' makgeolli (막걸리, rice wine), co-produced with Yangjojang Kkulkkulhouse.

properties.name: 정육면체
properties.description: This Michelin-recognized spot in Sinchon offers a vibrant, open-kitchen atmosphere perfect for a quick and satisfying meal. While the space is cozy and tables are close, the friendly staff and speedy service add to the lively vibe. It's a great place to experience well-executed noodle dishes, with a signature Hongtang (홍탕) beef noodle soup that's flavorful and not overly spicy, and a rich, nutty Sesame and Peanut Noodle (깨부수면) that's a unique treat. Don't miss their crispy, sweet-and-sour Yu Lin Gi (유린기) for a delightful contrast.

properties.name: JAEIN
properties.description: Nestled in a charming house in Hannam-dong, JAEIN is a sophisticated dessert bar that elevates the sweet experience. It's a place where traditional flavors meet innovative techniques, offering a curated selection of high-quality desserts, often paired with fine whiskies and brandies. The signature 'Namu' (나무) dessert, shaped like tree rings, is a must-try with its perfect balance of sweet and salty chocolate mousse and salted caramel, complemented by a crunchy praline finish. For a taste of spring, the limited edition 'Beotkkot Ending' (벚꽃엔딩) offers a delicate floral aroma with a refreshing berry tartness.

properties.name: 종점숯불갈비
properties.description:
Step into a piece of culinary history at Jongjeom Sutbul Galbi (종점숯불갈비), famously featured on the Japanese TV show 'The Solitary Gourmet'. While the restaurant shows its age, it retains the authentic charm seen on screen, complete with photos and autographs from celebrities. It's a pilgrimage site for fans of the show, offering a chance to sit at the very table where the protagonist dined.

While the grilled meats are a draw, don't miss the Ttuk Bulgogi (뚝불고기) – a hearty, flavorful stew of thinly sliced beef in a rich, sesame-flecked broth, brimming with vegetables and glass noodles. Paired with a bottle of soju (소주), it's a deeply satisfying meal that captures the essence of a classic Korean dining experience.

properties.name: 중화복춘
properties.description: Nestled in a charming alley of Hongdae, Junghwa Bokchun offers a unique dining experience with its distinct multi-level layout and private seating areas, creating an intimate Chinese ambiance. The service is exceptionally attentive, starting with helpful parking information and a personalized welcome card upon arrival. The meal begins with delightful appetizers like a spicy and sour pickled cabbage (짜사이) and a refreshing kelp and cabbage salad, complemented by fragrant jasmine tea. The star of the show is the signature Cotton Candy Cream Shrimp (목화솜 크림새우), featuring large, succulent shrimp coated in a light, crispy batter and tossed in a tangy, sweet cream sauce, balanced with fresh fruits and nuts. The staff's personal touch, like expertly mixing the sauce at your table, elevates the entire experience.

properties.name: 진가
properties.description: Step into Jin Ga (진가), a highly-acclaimed Chinese restaurant in Yeonnam-dong helmed by Chef Jin Saeng-yong, a culinary star from "Kang Ho Dae Gyeol Jung Hwa Dae Ban Jeom." This establishment, a recipient of the prestigious Blue Ribbon award for nine consecutive years, offers a refined yet approachable take on Chinese cuisine. While they focus on exquisite dishes rather than standard meals, the generous portions and high-quality ingredients make it a satisfying experience. The ambiance is clean and spacious, with a welcoming atmosphere thanks to the friendly staff.

properties.name: jowoo
properties.description: Nestled near Gwanghwamun and Gyeongbokgung stations, Jowoo offers an elegant Italian dining experience, perfect for a special evening out. The dinner course menu presents a delightful progression of flavors, starting with a refreshing seasonal fish ceviche (광어회) and warm bread. The highlight for many is the tender lamb or beef main course, beautifully complemented by a creamy potato salad. The meal concludes with a memorable dessert featuring ice cream and fruit puree, leaving you wanting more.

properties.name: 일광쪽갈비
properties.description: Ilkwang Jjokgalbi (일광쪽갈비) is a beloved neighborhood gem that has earned accolades from local residents and food critics alike, even receiving multiple Blue Ribbon awards since 2014. While some recent online reviews have mentioned service or portion size concerns, long-time patrons emphasize the consistent quality and the warm, family-like atmosphere. The star of the show is their signature jjokgalbi (쪽갈비), a simple yet perfectly executed dish that has kept customers coming back for years.

properties.name: 장군갈비
properties.description: Step into Janggun Galbi (장군갈비), a venerable institution with 40 years of history near Achasan Station. This beloved spot, recognized as a "Baeknyeon Gaje" (Centennial Store), exudes the charm of a classic Korean eatery, often frequented by local elders. While they offer a range of premium Hanwoo (Korean beef) cuts like raw galbi (생갈비) and raw flower sirloin (생꽃등심), their marinated galbi (양념갈비) and bulgogi (불고기) are also highly recommended, with many opting for the value-packed weekday lunch specials (점심특선).

properties.name: 장모님해장국
properties.description: Tucked away near the Gugi Tunnel, Jangmonim Haejangguk (장모님해장국) is a beloved spot for a unique take on Seonji Haejangguk (선지해장국), a hearty beef blood soup. While it boasts a cozy, old-school vibe with its red brick exterior and wooden interior, this restaurant is also a recognized favorite on shows like "Sung Si-kyung's Eating Well" (성시경 먹을텐데) and "Delicious Guys" (맛있는 녀석들). The star of the show is their signature Haejangguk, which stands out from the typical Yangpyeong style with its clean, refreshing broth enhanced by a spicy kick from gochugaru (고춧가루) rather than chili oil. Generous portions of tender tripe (양) and soft beef blood (선지) make this a truly satisfying meal, perfect for an early morning start or a late-night craving.

properties.name: 제일콩집
properties.description: This cozy spot near Taereungipgu Station is a haven for soy-based dishes, with a particularly celebrated Kongguksu (콩국수) that has recently started being served. The restaurant boasts a welcoming, traditional exterior and a spacious interior that fills up quickly, hinting at its popularity. Beyond their famous noodle dish, the menu offers a variety of tofu and Korean staples, complemented by fresh, house-made side dishes like their standout kimchi (김치).

properties.name: 이품
properties.description: Nestled in the heart of Yeonhui-dong, a trendy Seoul hotspot, Ipum is renowned as one of the city's top three Jjajangmyeon (짜장면) specialists. While the entrance might seem small, the interior stretches out, offering a surprisingly spacious dining experience. Despite its popularity, the high turnover means a short wait, usually around 10-15 minutes, is often all that's needed. Be aware that seating arrangements can sometimes lead to a brief wait even when tables are available, or you might be seated with other diners. The menu offers classic Chinese-Korean fare at reasonable prices, with many opting for the flavorful main dishes.

properties.name: 이조식탁
properties.description: Nestled slightly off the beaten path in the trendy Euljiro area, Ijo Sikdang offers a refined take on Korean home-style cooking. This restaurant, named after its owner-chef and his mother, specializes in beautifully presented *bansang* (반상) meals, featuring bibimbap (비빔밥) and *sotbap* (솥밥 - rice cooked in a hot pot). After 5 PM, the menu expands to include special dishes and traditional Korean *anjoo* (안주 - food to accompany alcohol), perfect for pairing with a selection of traditional liquors.

properties.name: WUI Tea House & Restaurant
properties.description: WUI Tea House & Restaurant in Yongsan offers an intriguing ambiance that beckons curious diners, though reservations are highly recommended, especially for dinner. The interior is beautifully designed, creating a sophisticated yet comfortable atmosphere. While many dishes require advance booking, the à la carte options are still worth exploring. The star of the show is the tasting course of Baijiu (고량주), featuring a selection of fragrant and complex spirits, perfect for those looking to explore beyond typical pairings. Don't miss the Jin Mi Ji (진미가지), a crispy fried eggplant dish with a delightful sweet and savory profile, enhanced by a surprising hint of Sichuan peppercorn.

properties.name: Your Dining
properties.description:
Nestled in the charming streets of Mangwon-dong, Your Dining is a Blue Ribbon Survey-certified gem offering authentic Italian cuisine. The vibe here is intimate and inviting, with a fully open kitchen where you can witness the chefs meticulously crafting each dish. They pride themselves on making everything from scratch, including fresh pasta, bread, sauces, and desserts, ensuring a truly homemade taste. 

The star of the show is their handmade pasta, with the Porcini Tayarin (포르치니 타야린) receiving high praise for its chewy, kal-guksu-like texture and rich mushroom flavor, perfectly complemented by a poached egg and cheese. Don't miss the "Your Fried Fish" (유어튀김), a crispy delight that pairs wonderfully with wine or beer, and the tender Grilled Scallops (관자구이), another excellent appetizer. Finish with their delightful Panna Cotta (판나코타) for a sweet ending.

properties.name: 이츠모라멘
properties.description: Nestled in the vibrant streets of Hongdae, Itsumo Ramen is a highly acclaimed spot, recognized for its five consecutive years of Blue Ribbon selection. Despite potential queues, the restaurant boasts an impressive turnover and ample seating, with convenient pre-ordering available via Coupang Eats. Don't miss their signature Itsumo Tonkotsu Ramen (이츠모 돈코츠 라멘), a rich and flavorful broth that's been compared to the iconic Ichiran Ramen experience, perfectly balanced for the Korean palate. They also offer a unique Tsukemen (츠케멘) where you can choose between a pork or chicken broth for dipping, and a surprisingly affordable small beer for just 1,500 KRW.

properties.name: 월강부산돼지국밥
properties.description:
Nestled in the vibrant Yeonnam-dong, Wolgang Busan Dwaeji Gukbap offers an authentic taste of Busan's signature pork soup. While the broth is clean and subtly flavored, reviewers highly recommend adding the provided *dadaegi* (다대기) for a more robust and refreshing taste. The *dwaeji gukbap* (돼지국밥) itself is hearty, featuring tender, lean pork and somen noodles, served with nutritious black rice. 

Beyond the comforting soup, this spot is also lauded as a *suyuk* (수육) haven. The thinly sliced boiled pork is incredibly tender and flavorful, making the *suyuk baekban* (수육백반) a must-try. Accompanied by a unique, tangy radish kimchi, the *suyuk* is so good that many suggest ordering it as a standalone dish on future visits.

properties.name: 유진막국수
properties.description: Located conveniently across from the Mondrian Hotel, Yujin Makguksu offers a welcoming atmosphere perfect for a casual meal. While the menu features options like jokbal (족발) and bossam (보쌈), the star of the show is their refreshing makguksu (막국수). Reviewers rave about the bossam kimchi (보쌈김치) and the overall deliciousness of both the bossam and makguksu, making it a great spot for hotel guests looking to explore local flavors.

properties.name: 우물집(구의점)
properties.description: Nestled near Guui Station, Umuljip offers a tranquil dining experience with its serene, bamboo-adorned interior and private rooms, perfect for special occasions or intimate dates. The star of the show is the hearty Hanu (한우) vegetable hot pot, served alongside fragrant, flavorful솥밥 (sotbap - stone pot rice). Don't miss the Goseari Pyogo (고사리 표고) sotbap, a delightful blend of fernbrake and shiitake mushrooms, or the unique Gondeure Tomato Basil (곤드레 토마토 바질) sotbap, offering a surprising yet harmonious fusion of flavors.

properties.name: 오일제
properties.description:
Tucked away in a quiet alley near Samgakji Station, Oillje (오일제) operates on a unique, highly curated system. This solo-run establishment focuses on a single, exquisite dish: perilla seed seaweed soup (들깨미역국). With only 50 servings available daily and limited operating hours, Oillje cultivates an air of exclusivity, making it a sought-after destination for those in the know. The chef, a veteran with 18 years of experience in Western cuisine, brings a meticulous approach to this Korean comfort food, ensuring quality and a memorable dining experience.

The signature dish, perilla seed seaweed soup (들깨미역국), is prepared with fresh seaweed harvested from Geumdo Island and a clean, refined beef bone broth. Each bowl is cooked to order, highlighting the delicate texture of the seaweed, and served with premium Koshihikari rice cooked in a traditional cauldron. The open kitchen allows diners to witness the precise culinary process, adding to the intimate and engaging atmosphere. This dedication to quality and a streamlined, chef-centric operation has created a buzz, making Oillje a must-visit for its unique concept and exceptional flavors.

properties.name: OSTERIA HYUN
properties.description: Nestled directly across from the Children's Grand Park entrance, Osteria Hyun offers a warm and inviting atmosphere, reminiscent of a cozy brick house. The space is thoughtfully designed with warm lighting and comfortable seating, creating a welcoming ambiance for all. A highlight is the authentic Italian wood-fired oven, where pizzas are expertly crafted using oak wood, adding a rustic charm to the dining experience. The restaurant's folding doors open to a terrace, seamlessly blending indoor and outdoor dining and offering a relaxed, almost resort-like feel, especially with the park just across the street.

properties.name: 용마갈비
properties.description: This bustling spot in Yongmasageori is a go-to for locals seeking a lively atmosphere perfect for group gatherings and company dinners. The star of the show is their signature Dwaeji Suje Pork Galbi (돼지수제 포크갈비), praised for its tender, well-marinated meat that's both sweet and savory. The attentive staff provides excellent grilling service, ensuring each bite is perfectly cooked. Don't miss the complimentary Mul Naengmyeon (물냉면), a refreshing palate cleanser that perfectly complements the rich flavors of the galbi.

properties.name: 용마해장국
properties.description:
Step into Yongma Haejangguk (용마해장국), a beloved local gem that has recently relocated to a cleaner, more spacious setting. This spot is renowned for its signature clear and refreshing bone soup (뼈해장국), packed with tender pork bones, fresh bean sprouts, hearty cabbage, and generous portions of blood sausage (seonji, 선지). The broth is so invigorating, it's said to cure any hangover, making it a true 'life-changing' haejangguk experience.

Don't miss their unique side dish, gochujang-ajji (고추장아찌) – pickled chili peppers in a savory paste. It's so addictive, you'll want to buy a jar to take home! The interior is simple and clean, allowing you to focus on the delicious food. While the portion of bones might have slightly decreased over time, the taste remains exceptional, with perfectly chewy meat and a broth that’s both clean and deeply satisfying. Add some chopped chili peppers for an extra kick!

properties.name: yosoojung
properties.description: Yosoojung is a hidden gem that truly celebrates the oyster in all its glory. This establishment participated in the 'Oh My Oyster' festival, showcasing a masterful and creative approach to this beloved bivalve. Expect a refined dining experience where oysters are the star, prepared in ways that highlight their natural flavors and textures, complemented by expertly chosen wine and whiskey pairings.

properties.name: 오복수산
properties.description: This modern Japanese and Korean eatery in Magok offers a clean and spacious dining experience, perfect for group gatherings or a casual date. The restaurant features private rooms and a well-lit interior, with convenient underground parking and easy access from Magoknaru Station. Guests can easily order via tablet, and the menu boasts a variety of dishes, including a recommended special course.

properties.name: Osteria Orzo
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet residential area of Hannam-dong, Osteria Orzo offers an intimate dining experience within its cozy, albeit compact, interior. Despite the unassuming exterior, the second floor is beautifully decorated, creating a sophisticated ambiance. This Michelin-starred gem is a popular choice, often fully booked, especially after recent media attention. Reservations are highly recommended, and valet parking is available for convenience.

properties.name: Yellow Bowl
properties.description: Yellow Bowl is a celebrated pasta haven, consistently recognized with Blue Ribbon awards for seven consecutive years. Located conveniently near Chungjeongno Station, the restaurant boasts a bright and sophisticated ambiance with an open kitchen, making it an ideal spot for intimate dates. The extensive wine list, paired with a diverse menu of pasta and dinner dishes, positions Yellow Bowl as a popular destination for both pasta enthusiasts and wine lovers.

properties.name: 오통영
properties.description:
Nestled in Ichon-dong, O-Tongyeong offers a taste of the coastal city of Tongyeong right in Seoul. This Michelin Bib Gourmand recipient is a popular spot, often drawing queues, and is conveniently located near attractions like the National Museum of Korea and Hangang Park. The interior is cozy, with tables close together, fostering a communal dining experience.

Signature dishes include the flavorful clam bibimbap (꼬막 비빔밥), featuring plump clams mixed with a savory sauce, and the spicy stir-fried octopus (낙지볶음) with its tender, chewy texture. For a unique option, try the mentaiko ochazuke (명란 차 밥), a comforting rice soup with savory cod roe, perfect for a lighter meal or when you crave something different.

properties.name: 오리지널시카고피자
properties.description: This Hongdae spot is a prime destination for a romantic date, boasting a bright and stylish interior with great views, perfect for enjoying a casual meal. The terrace seating is especially appealing for warmer days, ideal for pairing pizza with a refreshing beer. Don't miss their signature Chicago-style pizzas, piled high with gooey cheese and featuring fresh ingredients, with the Classic Tomato pizza loaded with mushrooms and pepperoni being a standout choice. The BBQ Fries, topped with beef, tomatoes, and sour cream, are also a highly recommended appetizer that pairs perfectly with their selection of beers, including Red Rock draft.

properties.name: 우동가조쿠
properties.description: This popular udon spot in Seongsu-dong is a must-visit for noodle lovers. Be prepared for a wait, as they operate on a first-come, first-served basis with no reservations, and all members of your party must be present to be seated. The menu offers a wide variety of hot and cold udon dishes, as well as donburi. While the exact signature dish is debated, the 'Yukyuk Udon' (육육우동) is a popular choice for those seeking a spicy kick, and sides like the 'Yubu Chobap' (유부초밥) and 'Gamja Goroke' (감자고로케) are highly recommended to complement your meal.

properties.name: 오향만두
properties.description: This bustling spot is a local favorite, often packed with diners, offering a lively atmosphere perfect for enjoying classic Chinese-Korean fare. The star of the show is their dumplings, with both the steamed (jjin-mandu, 찐만두) and pan-fried (gun-mandu, 군만두) versions receiving high praise for their tender, chewy texture and savory, well-seasoned filling. Don't miss the tangsuyuk (탕수육), a generous portion of crispy-yet-chewy fried pork with a distinctively tangy and sweet sauce reminiscent of old-school recipes.

properties.name: 우마이도
properties.description: A long-standing ramen institution since 2007, Umaydo (우마이도) is a popular spot near Konkuk University Station, often bustling with students. The vibe is casual and efficient, with ordering done via kiosk before entering. Their signature dish, the Shiro-maru ramen (시로마루 라멘), is noted for its rich tonkotsu (돈코츠) broth, with customizable noodle firmness. Reviewers rave about the tender chashu (차슈) and the satisfying depth of flavor, making it a must-try for ramen enthusiasts.

properties.name: 우성갈비
properties.description: Step into a classic Seoul eatery with a no-frills, old-school vibe, perfect for a casual night out. The star of the show is their signature marinated pork ribs (dwaeji galbi, 돼지갈비), which are expertly pre-grilled over charcoal before hitting your table. The marinade is subtly seasoned, allowing the tender, springy texture of the meat to shine through. Enjoy this with a refreshing side of spicy, crunchy chives (buchu muchim, 부추무침) for a truly satisfying meal.

properties.name: 우리밀국시
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Seongbuk-dong, Uri Mil Guksu offers a cozy dining experience with both traditional floor seating and standard tables. The restaurant boasts an impressive thirteen Blue Ribbon awards, hinting at its culinary excellence. Their signature dish, Guksu (국시), features incredibly soft, thin noodles that soak up a rich, savory beef broth. While the noodles might lack the chewiness some prefer, their melt-in-your-mouth texture is perfect for those seeking a delicate and comforting meal.

properties.name: AL'AISE
properties.description: Nestled in a serene, forest-like ambiance, AL'AISE offers a refined French dining experience that celebrates the freshest seasonal ingredients sourced locally in Korea. The restaurant lives up to its name, 'a l'aise,' meaning comfort, by presenting familiar flavors in an approachable yet sophisticated manner. Expect meticulously crafted dishes, from delicate amuse-bouches like the fried chicken mousse to expertly prepared mains such as the crispy-skinned mackerel (samchi, 삼치) with chewy barley, and the succulent Korean beef striploin (hanu chaekkeut, 한우 채끝) or flavorful duck (ori, 오리). Each course is a testament to the chef's skill in highlighting the natural essence of the ingredients, making it a perfect spot for special occasions or a tranquil escape.

properties.name: ART SPACE WOOWOL
properties.description:
Step into ArtSpace Woowol, a sophisticated hanwoo (한우) omakase restaurant tucked away in a quiet alley near Hongdae. The ambiance feels like an art gallery, with striking pieces adorning the walls and all seating at a bar overlooking a grill, perfect for an intimate date or a special occasion. It's an ideal spot for solo diners seeking a delicious meal in a comfortable setting.

The star of the show is the premium 1++ grade Korean beef, expertly grilled over charcoal right at your table. Beyond the exquisite grilled cuts, the omakase experience features creative dishes centered around hanwoo (한우), offering a unique culinary journey. For wine lovers, there's a complimentary corkage on one bottle of red or white wine per table.

properties.name: 안주마을
properties.description: Nestled in the charming Seochon area near Gyeongbokgung Station, Anju Village (안주마을) is a highly sought-after spot known for its incredible food and lively atmosphere. Be prepared for a wait, as this popular eatery often has a queue, especially for its coveted lunch slots. Once seated, dive into their must-try dishes like the exceptionally rich and fatty Southern Sea Bullet Squid Sashimi (남해 총알 한치회), which reviewers rave about. Other highlights include the savory Yeosu Minari and Clam Meat Pancake (여수 미나리 바지락살 전) and the flavorful Herring Roe with Tofu (청어알젓과 두부).

properties.name: 애성회관
properties.description: This spot, famously praised by Yoo Jae-suk on the show 'Teummannamyeon', offers a classic, old-school vibe reminiscent of a long-standing neighborhood eatery. Specializing in premium Korean beef (hanwoo, 한우), their signature dish is the clear broth beef soup, hanwoo gomtang (한우곰탕). They pride themselves on using only the highest grade of Korean beef, even offering a tenfold refund if their claim isn't met. The menu also features other Korean favorites like suyuk (수육) and nakji bokkeum (낙지볶음).

properties.name: Artisan Bakers
properties.description: Escape the desert heat and step into a lush, indoor garden oasis at Artisan Bakers. This popular spot, a true 'hot place' in Dubai, offers a refreshing respite with its cool, green ambiance. Indulge in a delightful array of freshly baked goods, from decadent cakes to a wide selection of breads, all beautifully presented with fresh fruit. Don't miss exploring their separate, chilled cheese and charcuterie room for a unique sensory experience.

properties.name: N Grill
properties.description: Perched atop N Seoul Tower, N Grill offers a truly elevated dining experience with breathtaking panoramic views of Seoul. This revolving restaurant provides a romantic ambiance, making it a classic spot for special occasions and proposals, as evidenced by the "Would you marry me?" messages often seen on its windows. Guests can savor a multi-course tasting menu, with highlights like the tender Hanwoo (한우) sirloin and refreshing seasonal amuse-bouches, all while watching the cityscape transform from day to night.

properties.name: 영덕회식당
properties.description: A 40-year veteran in Chungmuro, Yeongdeok Hoesikdang is renowned as the originator of makhoe (막회) in Seoul, a dish where fresh sashimi is mixed with vegetables and a special spicy-sweet gochujang sauce. While makhoe is a year-round favorite, winter brings the highly anticipated gwamegi (과메기), a delicacy of dried herring. The restaurant's signature gochujang sauce, infused with scallions, sesame, garlic, and chili, is the secret ingredient that elevates both dishes, offering a refreshing and addictive kick. Don't miss the comforting bean sprout soup and the option to mix leftover makhoe with rice and freshly pressed sesame oil for a satisfying finish.

properties.name: Everest
properties.description: Everest Yangjae is a bustling Nepalese and Indian curry house, popular with locals and colleagues alike. The interior is spacious with plenty of tables for groups, and the open kitchen is run by a friendly local couple. The lunch set is a great value, featuring three different curries, rice, naan, tandoori chicken, and a refreshing lassi (라씨). It's a fantastic spot to explore a variety of authentic flavors.

properties.name: SUSHIKASE
properties.description: Step into a world of elevated conveyor belt sushi at SUSHIKASE, located in the bustling basement food court of Lotte Department Store in Myeongdong. This spot offers a sophisticated dining experience, perfect for a delightful treat. The star of the show is the luxurious gold plate featuring beef tartare (yukhoe, 육회) topped with salmon, a truly decadent combination. Another standout is the uni (sea urchin, 성게알) wrapped in gamtae (감태), elevated by a hint of truffle for an aromatic finish.

properties.name: Sailer
properties.description: Step into Sailer (싸일러) and feel like you've been transported to a charming Austrian bakery, right in the heart of Yongsan. This popular spot buzzes with locals from early morning, offering a unique European-style experience with its art-adorned walls and a delightful array of pastries. While the sandwiches look tempting, many patrons rave about the comforting soups, perfect for dipping into their freshly baked bread.

properties.name: 수유갈비산성골
properties.description: This beloved local spot, a fixture in the Suyu area for over 30 years, is renowned for its exceptional value and authentic Korean barbecue. They specialize in prime cuts of pork, meticulously aged for 345 hours to achieve peak flavor and tenderness. The signature dish is the '345 Saeng-Dwaeji Galbi' (345 생돼지갈비), made from the most desirable ribs (3rd to 5th bone), offering a truly superior texture and taste. The restaurant boasts a unique, patented 'Tongdori' (통돌이) system that pre-grills the meat to perfection, allowing diners to relax and enjoy their meal without the hassle of cooking.

properties.name: 수정궁
properties.description: Step into Sujeong Gung (수정궁), a beloved dumpling haven in Dobong-gu that has been serving locals since 1988. This no-frills eatery, often bustling with long-time patrons and even those who travel from afar, offers a focused menu that truly lets the dumplings shine. Expect a cozy, intimate setting, and be prepared for a potential wait during peak hours, a testament to its enduring popularity. It's a place where the simple act of enjoying a perfectly crafted dumpling is the main event.

properties.name: 서촌프렌치
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet Seochon alley, Moulin offers an immersive French dining experience with its distinctive dark exterior and antique interior. The walls are adorned with the chef's personal collection of pepper mills, adding a unique visual flair. Chef Yoon Yerang masterfully blends seasonal Korean ingredients with classic French techniques, evident in dishes like the eel p Tiet (장어 피티비에) and octopus salad. The tasting menu, featuring nine courses, showcases a thoughtful balance of delicate and bold flavors, making it a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: CHEF. JUNGHYUN
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet corner of NoMad, New York, Atomix is a culinary gem that reinterprets Korean flavors with sophisticated artistry. This intimate, 14-seat counter dining experience offers a journey through Korean cuisine, where each of the 12 courses in the tasting menu is a meticulously crafted work of art. Chef Junghyun Park masterfully blends traditional Korean techniques like fermentation and the use of seasonal ingredients with a modern, avant-garde approach, creating dishes that are both deeply flavorful and visually stunning, often highlighting the delicate essence of seafood and the elegance of Korean culinary traditions.

properties.name: SECOND COFFEE
properties.description: Tucked away in a neighborhood brimming with cafes, Second Coffee stands out for its truly exceptional coffee. Reviewers rave about their latte, calling it one of the best they've had in Seoul. The cafe boasts a sophisticated and stylish interior with warm, woody tones and subtle lighting, creating a comfortable atmosphere. Its convenient location near Seosunra-gil and Changdeokgung Palace makes it an ideal spot to relax and recharge.

properties.name: 수락이오냉면
properties.description: A beloved local spot near Suraksan Station, Surak Io Naengmyeon has transformed from a nostalgic childhood haunt into a modern, bustling eatery. While the interior has been significantly updated, the heart of the restaurant remains its dedication to delicious, meticulously prepared dishes. Expect a short wait, as this popular establishment often has a queue, but the efficient service and the promise of flavorful naengmyeon (냉면) make it worthwhile. The menu has expanded, offering a variety of creative options alongside their classic offerings.

properties.name: Chef Tainer
properties.description: Chef Tainer offers a sophisticated fine dining experience in the heart of Itaewon, perfect for special occasions. While the uphill walk from the station might be a bit of a climb, the restaurant's elegant interior, with options for private rooms, and the attentive service create a luxurious atmosphere. The 8-course dinner, featuring dishes like squid ink risotto arancini and a refreshing abalone timbale, showcases the chef's skill and attention to presentation, making it a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: 성수속향연
properties.description:
Step into Seongsu Sokhyangyeon and be greeted by a surprisingly modern and chic ambiance, a stark contrast to its unassuming exterior. This popular spot, often chosen over pasta joints, buzzes with a diverse crowd of office workers, couples, and families, hinting at its widespread appeal. Start your culinary journey with refreshing jasmine tea and a trio of crisp side dishes, including a delightful pickled cabbage that cuts through richness perfectly. 

The signature dishes are where Seongsu Sokhyangyeon truly shines. The Hyangyeon Buchu Haemul Jjamppong (향연 부추 해물 짬뽕) boasts a deep, flavorful broth brimming with fresh seafood and a generous amount of chives (buchu), offering a complex taste that's both spicy and savory without being heavy. Equally impressive is the Hyangyeon Jjajangmyeon (향연 짜장면), a unique take on the classic. Made with natural moisture from vegetables and meat, the sauce is rich, velvety, and clings beautifully to the perfectly chewy noodles, delivering a satisfying flavor that never becomes monotonous.

properties.name: 송화산시도삭면
properties.description: Step into Songhwa Shanxi Daodao Noodles (송화산시도삭면) 2nd branch in the vibrant Konkuk University area for a surprisingly modern and chic dining experience. Unlike typical Chinese restaurants, this spot boasts a clean, bright, and stylish interior, making it a comfortable place to relax and enjoy a meal or drinks. The highlight is watching the chefs skillfully shave the noodles directly from a dough ball into boiling water, a mesmerizing process that promises a unique culinary adventure.

properties.name: 성북동빵공장
properties.description:
Nestled in the scenic Seongbuk-dong, this bakery cafe is a must-visit for anyone embarking on a Seoul bread pilgrimage. It's a popular spot, especially after a stroll through nearby Waryong Park, offering a cozy ambiance perfect for enjoying a warm drink and a vast selection of baked goods. While the cafe boasts ample parking with valet service, be aware that some visitors have reported unfriendly parking attendants.

The star of the show is undoubtedly the incredible variety of breads and pastries. From delicate macarons and rich chocolate mousse cakes to the highly recommended Pandoro (팡도르), a light and airy 'bread of gold,' there's a delightful treat for every palate. The cafe also offers a range of coffee options, including unique blends, making it an ideal destination for a leisurely break.

properties.name: 소바쿠
properties.description: This cozy spot near Achasan Station is a popular destination, often with a queue, for delicious soba (소바) and udon (우동). The interior has an intimate, Japanese-inspired vibe with limited seating, contributing to its charm and sometimes longer wait times. They offer both cold and hot soba year-round, with udon joining the menu as a winter special from October to March.

properties.name: center coffee
properties.description: Center Coffee is a minimalist haven for coffee purists, focusing on the essence of the bean from sourcing to brewing. Its calm and clean ambiance makes it an ideal spot for solo contemplation or a peaceful break. Whether you're seeking a meticulously crafted espresso or a refreshing cold brew, this cafe offers a moment of quiet indulgence amidst the bustling city.

properties.name: 소이연남
properties.description: This bustling Thai noodle shop, originating from Seoul's Yeonnam-dong, offers an authentic taste of Thailand right in the heart of the city. Be prepared for a wait, especially during weekday lunch, as this popular spot is known for its flavorful Thai beef noodle soup (소고기 쌀국수). The rich and deep broth is a must-try, and you can customize your experience with various condiments like fish sauce, sugar, chili powder, and chili vinegar.

properties.name: 서촌계단집
properties.description: Nestled in a charming alley near Gyeongbokgung Station, Seochon Gyedan House (서촌계단집) is a bustling seafood haven that requires patience but rewards with fresh, delicious catches. Expect a wait, especially on weekends, as they only seat complete parties. Once inside, the atmosphere is lively and the menu, though sometimes limited by availability, offers gems like the sweet and savory Gizzard Shad (전어회) and incredibly tender Whole Steamed Squid (오징어 통찜). Don't miss the flavorful Seafood Ramyeon (해물라면), a perfect end to a satisfying meal.

properties.name: 설가온
properties.description: Nestled in the Sejong Center for the Performing Arts, Seolgayon is a highly recommended destination for formal occasions, particularly family meetings (sanggyeonrye - 상견례). Its prime location near Gwanghwamun Station offers convenient access, even on rainy days. The restaurant boasts a traditional Korean hanok (한옥) ambiance with private rooms accommodating various group sizes, ensuring a quiet and intimate dining experience. Seolgayon is recognized for its elegant presentation and delicious cuisine, making it a top choice for special gatherings and impressing guests.

properties.name: 서부감자국
properties.description: This long-standing local gem in Eunpyeong-gu is a must-visit for its hearty gamjatang (감자탕). The signature Ugeoji Gamjatang (우거지 감자탕), featuring tender pork ribs and generous portions of dried napa cabbage, is a standout. The clear, flavorful broth is perfect for enjoying with a side of their famously delicious kimchi (김치) or for adding ramen noodles (라면사리) to create a richer stew. Don't leave without trying the flavorful fried rice (볶음밥), a perfect finish to this satisfying meal.

properties.name: 성산왕갈비
properties.description: Step into a Seoul institution where the owner, a distinguished gentleman with neatly swept white hair, personally grills your meat to perfection. This long-standing establishment, operating for over 25 years, is renowned for its exceptional saenggalbi (생갈비), or unmarinated ribs. The quality is so high that even the pork fat is a revelation, boasting an incredible texture and flavor. Complementing the star dish are meticulously prepared, 100% domestic side dishes, including a standout anchovy side dish (myeolchi-jorim, 멸치조림) and crisp lettuce, all designed to enhance the main event.

properties.name: 성수족발
properties.description: This popular spot, often hailed as one of Seoul's top three jokbal (족발) joints, draws crowds for its unique take on the classic dish. While the flavor profile leans sweet rather than savory, many find themselves hooked after the first bite. The jokbal (족발) boasts a wonderfully chewy texture and is remarkably tender, with no gamey smell. It's a dish that might surprise you with its sweetness, but the accompanying banchan (반찬), like the spicy radish salad (무채) and bean sprouts (콩나물), offer a perfect salty counterpoint, making for a delightful combination.

properties.name: 성너머집
properties.description: Nestled at the foot of Bukhansan mountain, Seongneomeojip is a legendary 55-year-old establishment renowned for its rustic, home-style chicken dishes. The star of the show is the dakbokkeumtang (닭볶음탕), a rich and savory braised chicken stew with a thick, deeply flavorful broth that has been simmered for hours. The generous portions of tender chicken and surprisingly large, soft potatoes make this a must-try for anyone seeking authentic Korean comfort food, especially after a hike.

properties.name: 속초생태집
properties.description: Step into a beloved old-school eatery, a Blue Ribbon recipient for a decade, that exudes a classic Seoul vibe. While the space is cozy and often bustling with office workers and tourists, especially during dinner, the atmosphere is warm and inviting, perfect for intimate gatherings. Don't miss their signature saengtaetang (생태탕), a clear, refreshing, and subtly spicy soup brimming with fresh pollack. For a heartier evening meal, the assorted grilled platter (모듬구이) is highly recommended for its delicious flavors.

properties.name: 성북동누룽지백숙
properties.description: Nestled on a charming hillside, this unassuming eatery offers a comforting and hearty dining experience. The star of the show is the signature Nurungji Baek숙 (누룽지 백숙), a whole chicken stuffed with glutinous rice porridge, served atop a unique pot filled with crispy scorched rice (nurungji, 누룽지) and more porridge. The chicken is tender and easy to eat, while the scorched rice and porridge combination is a savory and satisfying finish. Don't miss the Pye-myong-jeon (메밀전), a buckwheat pancake elevated by fresh spring mountain vegetables, offering a delightful contrast in texture.

properties.name: 삼청동수제비
properties.description: A long-standing institution since 1982, Samcheongdong Sujebi is a Michelin-recognized gem in Seoul, known for its hearty and comforting Korean comfort food. Despite the bustling crowds and potential waits, the restaurant's efficient turnover and traditional, welcoming atmosphere make it a worthwhile stop. Popular dishes include their signature sujebi (수제비), a chewy noodle soup, and the rich, nutty ongsimi (옹심이), a glutinous rice ball soup, both perfectly complemented by their refreshing kimchi (김치) and yeolmu kimchi (열무김치).

properties.name: Brewing ceremony
properties.description: Nestled in the charming alleys of Seongsu-dong, Brewing Ceremony is a hidden gem that lives up to its name with a meticulous approach to coffee. This roastery cafe boasts a chic, minimalist interior where the star is undoubtedly the coffee beans, displayed like art. They offer a curated selection of hand-drip coffees, with each bean's unique flavor profile poetically described on charming cards, allowing you to choose your brew based on evocative notes like 'a relaxing commute' or 'first love.' The dedication to quality and the unique sensory experience make it a must-visit for coffee aficionados.

properties.name: Bistro Chin Chin
properties.description: Nestled near Cheong Wa Dae, Bistro Chin Chin charms with its exterior resembling a quaint, old-world European home, complete with a friendly feline greeter. Inside, the ambiance is cozy and inviting, bathed in natural light, making it a perfect spot for a relaxed date. The menu offers a delightful array of dishes, with the plump and chewy octopus bisque (문어 비스큐) and the rich sea urchin, shrimp, and squid ink risotto (성게알, 새우, 오징어 먹물 리조또) standing out as particularly memorable. The mentaiko and mullet roe pasta (명란&숭어알 파스타), featuring a unique medley of vegetables, adds a fresh and exciting twist to the dining experience.

properties.name: via merengue
properties.description: This vibrant spot in Sinchon is a must-visit for authentic Latin American flavors, boasting an impressive five consecutive Blue Ribbon awards. Be prepared for a potential wait, as this cozy eatery is popular and reservations are highly recommended. The menu, ordered via QR code, features delicious options like the flavorful tacos, which are surprisingly approachable even with cilantro, and a satisfyingly warm and savory burrito that makes for a perfect meal.

properties.name: brera
properties.description: Nestled near Beoti-gogae Station, Brera is a charming Italian eatery with a vibrant red exterior, recognized by Blue Ribbon and past Michelin selections. The spacious interior, accented with red, offers a comfortable setting perfect for group gatherings or business lunches, featuring large windows that let in ample natural light. With an extensive menu of pizzas, pastas, and more, all described in English, it's clear Brera caters to a diverse clientele.

properties.name: 산울림1992
properties.description: Sanullim 1992 is a renowned haven for traditional Korean alcohol (jeontongju, 전통주) enthusiasts, frequently featured in media. This Hongdae gem boasts an astonishing selection of over 2,500 varieties of jeontongju from across the country. A standout feature is their tasting flight, offering three glasses of the day's featured drinks for a modest price, allowing patrons to discover new favorites. The menu offers refined Korean dishes like the tender Arongsatae Suyuk Naengchae (아롱사태 수육냉채) and flavorful Naejangjjim (내장찜), perfect for pairing with their extensive drink list.

properties.name: Pakito
properties.description: Pakito

properties.name: 빠오즈푸
properties.description: A long-standing favorite near Children's Grand Park Station, Paozifu (빠오즈푸) has been serving up delicious dumplings for over a decade. This spot, which has even been featured on the popular TV show 'Master of Living' (생활의 달인), is known for its freshly made baozi (빠오즈). The thin, delicate wrappers encase tender, flavorful fillings, and the dumplings are celebrated for their incredible juiciness, making them a must-try for any dumpling enthusiast.

properties.name: Parc
properties.description: Parc offers a unique dining experience that blends the industrial charm of Seoul's Yongsan district with meticulous craftsmanship. The atmosphere is alive with the sounds and sights of metalwork, creating a surprisingly rhythmic and engaging backdrop to your meal. It’s a place where the precision of welding meets the art of culinary creation, resulting in a truly memorable and stable experience.

properties.name: BUDDHA'S BELLY
properties.description: Step into Buddha's Belly for an authentic Thai culinary journey right in the heart of Seoul's Express Bus Terminal. This upscale eatery offers a sophisticated ambiance, perfect for a delightful meal. Their set menus are a fantastic value, offering a curated selection of their best dishes. Don't miss the crispy and flavorful Thod Mun Goong (텃만꿍), a delightful shrimp fritter, and the rich, aromatic Poo Pad Pong Curry (푸팟퐁커리) served with fluffy white rice, creating an addictive combination.

properties.name: 봉화묵집
properties.description: Nestled near Jeongneung Arirang Market, Bonghwamukjip offers a cozy, home-like atmosphere that feels like stepping into a traditional Korean house. This beloved spot, run by a grandmotherly figure who personally crafts every dish, has maintained its charm over the years, evident in its unchanged interior and the continued presence of handmade dumplings (mandu, 만두). While prices have seen modest increases, the quality and authenticity remain, with a special mention for their signature slightly fermented, refreshing kimchi (김치) and the versatile, savory seasoning paste that enhances every bite.

properties.name: 북악정
properties.description: Nestled near Bugakjeong in Pyeongchang-dong, Dure You offers a refined Korean fine dining experience. The restaurant, recognized by the Michelin Guide, provides a serene and spacious interior, perfect for an intimate meal. Guests rave about the 'Byeol' (별) course menu, with particular highlights including a creamy, savory 'tarakjuk' (타락죽) reminiscent of a rich cream soup, and a refreshing seasonal 'chimchae' (침채) featuring thinly sliced kohlrabi with a yuzu dressing. The presentation is as exquisite as the flavors, making for a memorable culinary journey.

properties.name: 산골막국수
properties.description:
A beloved makguksu (막국수) spot has returned to Euljiro 4-ga, now in a new building at the same location. While the menu remains similar, featuring their signature buckwheat noodles and a hearty nokdujeon (녹두전), the atmosphere has shifted from its former old-school charm. The makguksu is served bibim-style with broth on the side, topped with seasoned sauce, pear, and shredded chicken, offering a subtly seasoned flavor that some might find a bit mild. 

This revived eatery, now operating later hours and connected to a hof (호프) pub, offers a taste of nostalgia for those who remember its past. It's a place to revisit familiar flavors in a refreshed setting, perfect for a casual meal or a late-night bite in the bustling Euljiro area.

properties.name: Shanti
properties.description: Step into Shanti and be transported to India with its vibrant decor and authentic flavors. While the tandoori chicken (탄두리치킨) might be a bit plain on its own, it's the perfect complement when mixed into their flavorful lamb masala curry (양고기 마살라 커리). Don't miss the chicken chow mein (치킨차우민), a surprisingly addictive noodle dish that bridges Thai and American-Chinese influences, and be sure to order extra butter naan (버터난) to soak up every last drop of deliciousness.

properties.name: 불이아
properties.description: Step into Bul-i-a (불이아) in Jongno and be transported to a charming Chinese eatery, complete with traditional decor and a comfortably spacious ambiance. This spot is perfect for intimate dinners or lively gatherings, offering private rooms for a more exclusive feel. The attentive staff and well-spaced tables ensure a pleasant dining experience, making it an ideal destination for both casual meals and special occasions.

properties.name: mils
properties.description: Step into Mils (밀스) and be greeted by a striking display of oak logs, hinting at the culinary magic within. This bakery cafe is renowned for its signature sausage buns, crafted with passion and a commitment to flavor. The secret? Sausages are grilled over charcoal made from real oak wood, infusing them with a deep, smoky aroma that elevates every bite. From the classic Bratwurst bun (브랏부어스트 번) with its satisfying snap to the surprisingly delightful Neapolitan bun (나폴리탄 번), a unique fusion of pasta and bread, there's a bun to tantalize every taste bud.

properties.name: BIRCH coffee
properties.description: Transport yourself to the heart of New York City with Birch Coffee, a specialty coffee shop that first opened its doors in NYC in 2009 before arriving in Seoul's trendy Seongsu-dong. The cafe boasts a minimalist, industrial-chic interior with exposed concrete and black accents, reminiscent of a New York loft. It's the perfect spot to enjoy a quiet moment, with ample natural light, especially on the second floor, making it ideal for work or relaxation. You can even find iconic New York-themed decor, including cityscapes and familiar paper cups, adding to the authentic vibe.

properties.name: bohey coffee
properties.description: Tucked away in a quiet alley near Yaksu Station, Bohey Coffee is a cozy haven that feels like a hidden gem. The cafe's charm is amplified by the owner's handmade ceramics, which are displayed throughout the space, adding a unique artistic touch. It's the perfect spot for a peaceful escape, offering a tranquil atmosphere ideal for quiet contemplation or a relaxed chat. Be sure to note the two-hour seating limit, perfect for those seeking a brief, delightful respite.

properties.name: bamaself
properties.description: Bamaself is a beloved coffee shop in Yongsan-gu, helmed by a seasoned barista with a history of successful cafes in Hongdae and Hannam-dong. Despite its cozy, intimate space, the atmosphere is warm and inviting, making it a go-to spot for coffee aficionados. The owner's dedication to their craft shines through in every cup, creating a loyal following who eagerly anticipate their expertly brewed beverages.

properties.name: meal°
properties.description: Located near the Seoul Station Airport Railroad, this spot offers a premium kimbap (김밥) experience tailored for travelers. The star is the "Seoul Meal" kimbap, distinguished by its generous fillings of avocado, thick egg omelet, and savory beef, all presented in a neat, sealed container. It's designed for easy, mess-free eating on the go, making it a convenient and balanced meal option before a flight or long journey, complete with a side of refreshing kkakdugi (깍두기).

properties.name: 미스터서왕만두
properties.description:
This beloved spot in Sinchon is a must-visit, especially after an escape room adventure. The vibe is casual and perfect for sharing with friends, with a self-serve bar for pickles and banchan (반찬). While the prices have increased since the reviewer's school days, they still feel reasonable for the quality and quantity.

The star of the show is undoubtedly the dumplings. The xiaolongbao (소룡포) are bursting with juicy broth, but the real showstoppers are the gunmandu (군만두) and shrimp dumplings (새우만두). The gunmandu are praised for their incredible crispiness, while the shrimp dumplings offer a satisfying chew with real shrimp inside. Don't miss the clear, refreshing seafood soup (해물탕) either – it's a perfect, light accompaniment to the rich dumplings and a cold beer.

properties.name: 베이커리봉교
properties.description: Nestled in a quiet alley away from the bustling Mangwon Market, Bakery Bonggyo is a beloved bakery with a decade-long legacy, even earning a spot in the Blue Ribbon Survey for nine consecutive years. Originally from Sangsu, this popular spot has reopened in Mangwon, quickly gaining traction for its delicious offerings. While many items sell out fast, especially their signature honey baguette (꿀바게트) and milk cream bread (우유크림빵), the selection includes delightful cookies, pastries like galettes and sablés, and various baked goods perfect with coffee.

properties.name: VERA PIZZA NAPOLI
properties.description: Nestled in the trendy Itaewon district, Vera Pizza Napoli offers an authentic Neapolitan pizza experience, boasting certification from the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN). The ambiance is chic and modern, with an open kitchen allowing diners to witness the pizza-making artistry. While prices are on the higher side, the quality of ingredients and the skill of the pizzaiolo shine through, making it a standout choice for pizza aficionados.

properties.name: VATOS
properties.description: Step into VATOS and feel the vibrant, energetic atmosphere that transports you straight to a lively cantina. This spot is perfect for group gatherings or a fun date night, with its spacious layout and mood lighting creating an ideal setting for conversation and drinks. Get ready for a fusion fiesta where Korean flavors meet Mexican flair, making it a unique culinary adventure.

properties.name: Beijingkoya
properties.description: Craving the crispy skin of Peking duck (북경오리) after a trip to China? Beijingkoya offers an authentic taste of this iconic dish, with a focus on the incredibly satisfying crunch of the duck's skin. While the tender meat offers a familiar flavor profile similar to smoked duck, it's the perfectly roasted exterior that truly shines. It's a place to indulge in the full Peking duck experience, a welcome treat for those who found their portion too small on their travels.

properties.name: MUTIN
properties.description: MUTIN offers a relaxed yet stylish ambiance, perfect for intimate gatherings and special occasions. The interior complements their extensive natural wine list, with a sommelier ready to guide you through unique selections based on your preferences and food pairings. The menu, which changes with the seasons, highlights fresh ingredients, including vegetables grown on their own farm, offering a delightful culinary journey.

properties.name: MANGIONE
properties.description: Tucked away in the basement of the Sinyongsan Station complex, Mangione (만지오네) offers an intimate omakase experience helmed by Chef Jeong Ik-sang. This reservation-only spot, easily accessible via the subway, presents a curated tasting menu that changes with the seasons. Expect a delightful journey through refined dishes, from plump Tongyeong oysters (통영 굴) and sweet scallop carpaccio (홍가리비찜) to the surprising melon and prosciutto pairing, and a rich chichetti (치케티) that evokes Venetian street food. The meal culminates with expertly grilled Korean beef steak (한우 스테이크), leaving a lasting impression of sophisticated flavors.

properties.name: MANIMAL SMOKE HOUSE
properties.description: Step into MANIMAL SMOKE HOUSE and be greeted by the irresistible aroma of wood smoke, a perfect ambiance for any occasion, especially during the festive year-end season. This popular Itaewon spot is renowned for its authentic American-style barbecue platters, featuring tender, smoke-infused meats like pulled pork and spare ribs. Pair your meal with their delicious craft beers for a truly satisfying experience that will have you coming back for more.

properties.name: 몽중식
properties.description: Step into a unique dining experience at Mongjung Sik, where each season unfolds a new cinematic theme, weaving a captivating story through a multi-course meal. This year's December theme, inspired by 'Love Actually,' transforms the space into a festive wonderland, perfect for year-end celebrations. The intimate, reservation-only setting ensures a private and immersive atmosphere, making it ideal for enjoying the thoughtfully crafted dishes that complement the ongoing narrative.

properties.name: MYEONGDONG PIZZA
properties.description: Step into Myeongdong Pizza for a surprisingly delightful Italian experience in the heart of Seoul's historic district. With a clean and modern interior, this spot offers a refreshing ambiance that belies its traditional Myeongdong roots. It's a perfect setting for a casual meal with friends or even a classic first date, offering a menu that goes beyond expectations.

properties.name: MOTOR CITY
properties.description:
Motor City in Itaewon offers a vibrant, bustling atmosphere perfect for a casual meal with friends. While the menu has evolved, the signature Jackson 5 pizza, a half-and-half creation with Sweet Corn Basil, remains a popular choice. Don't miss the Buffalo Wings, described as a tangy, authentic American taste that perfectly complements a cold drink, and the Crispy Mushroom, a surprisingly juicy and flavorful appetizer.

The restaurant is a go-to spot for those craving a taste of American-style pizza and wings. The Jackson 5 (잭슨 5) pizza, customizable with a half-and-half option, is a must-try, alongside the zesty Buffalo Wings (버팔로윙) that pack a flavorful punch without being overly spicy. For a lighter, yet equally satisfying option, the Crispy Mushroom (크리스피 머쉬룸) offers a delightful contrast of textures and a creamy garlic dipping sauce that's perfect for sharing.

properties.name: MOZZA
properties.description: MOZZA is a beloved neighborhood gem in Seongbuk-dong, consistently drawing crowds and often requiring a wait, especially during peak hours. This cozy, home-like restaurant, recognized in the Blue Ribbon Survey from 2019-2024, offers a warm and inviting atmosphere perfect for family gatherings and relaxed meals. The open kitchen provides a lively glimpse into the culinary action, adding to the restaurant's charm.

properties.name: Moulin
properties.description:
Nestled in the charming Seochon neighborhood, Moulin offers an exquisite French dining experience, recognized by the Michelin Guide. This restaurant is perfect for special occasions, providing a sophisticated ambiance for a memorable date. The tasting menu is a journey through artfully crafted dishes, from the delicate amuse-bouche to the rich duck with foie gras and cherry, and the perfectly cooked lamb with autumnal flavors.

The culinary team showcases impressive technique, balancing textures and flavors with precision. Highlights include the succulent monkfish with okra and seaweed, the crispy yet tender shrimp with fig and rhubarb, and the earthy mushroom course. The meal concludes with a refreshing apple and cider palate cleanser and delicate petit fours, ensuring a seamless and delightful progression of tastes from start to finish.

properties.name: 맛이차이나
properties.description: A beloved Chinese restaurant that couples have frequented since their dating days, Matsi China offers a comforting and familiar dining experience. The menu boasts a wide array of delicious dishes, with the "Eohyang Gaji Saeu" (어향가지 새우) – crispy fried eggplant sandwiches filled with succulent shrimp – and the flavorful, seafood-rich Jjamppong (짬뽕) being standout choices. Portions are generous, and the attentive service, like splitting dishes without being asked, adds to the welcoming atmosphere.

properties.name: 만족오향족발
properties.description: This Michelin-recognized spot is a must-visit for any serious foodie exploring Seoul. Be prepared for a wait, as this popular establishment, often cited as one of Seoul's top three jokbal (족발) restaurants, draws crowds eager for their signature dish. The lively atmosphere buzzes with anticipation, reflecting its esteemed reputation and consistent accolades.

properties.name: 명동돈가스
properties.description:
A long-standing favorite in Myeongdong since 1983, this tonkatsu (돈까스) spot offers a classic Japanese-style experience. The star is their freshly fried pork cutlet, prepared with a special egg batter and breadcrumbs, ensuring a crispy exterior and juicy interior. While the minimalist side dishes of cabbage salad and pickled radish are simple, the attentive staff provides excellent service, even offering to warm up your rice and soup on chilly days and assisting foreign visitors with multilingual menus and basic English.

This restaurant is a popular choice for both locals and tourists, often recommended for those seeking a satisfying tonkatsu meal. The thick-cut pork cutlet is a generous portion, though some international visitors have noted that a larger size option would be appreciated. The accompanying sauces, one for dipping the cutlet and another for the salad, are perfectly balanced, and refills for cabbage are readily available.

properties.name: 루리커피
properties.description: Step into Ruri Coffee (루리커피) for a unique experience where virtual YouTubers (버튜버) might just be serving your drink! This spot is a haven for collectors and gamers, boasting a wide array of gachapon machines and figures. While the coffee is noted as delicious, be prepared for a premium price point, especially for their specialty pour-over coffee, which is an experience in itself.

properties.name: RECOVERY COFFEE BAR
properties.description: Escape the hustle and bustle of everyday life at Recovery Coffee Bar, a serene sanctuary in Seongsu-dong. This tranquil spot offers a much-needed respite, living up to its name by providing a space to recharge and recenter. Settle into a window seat and savor their unique signature drinks, like the 'Mannyonseol' (만년설) with its velvety cream and balanced bittersweet notes, or the exotic 'Sinbad's Latte' (신밧드의 라떼) that sparks the imagination with its complex flavors. It's the perfect place to find your calm amidst the city's energy.

properties.name: LOOKBACK COFFEE&ROASTERS
properties.description: Tucked away in the charming Yeonhui-dong, LOOKBACK COFFEE&ROASTERS feels like a hidden gem. This cozy, intimate space is adorned with tasteful art and quirky trinkets, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere. The owner's personal touch is evident throughout, from the curated decor to the thoughtfully arranged seating, which offers a comfortable distance between tables, perfect for quiet conversations. It's the ideal spot to unwind with a meticulously brewed cup of coffee, especially if you visit on a weekday for a truly peaceful experience.

properties.name: L'ESTIU
properties.description: Nestled in the charming alleyways of Hannam-dong, L'ESTIU offers a sophisticated yet welcoming ambiance, reminiscent of Tokyo's Omotesando. The restaurant, a beautifully remodeled house, boasts a meticulously maintained garden that sets a tranquil tone upon arrival. Inside, the clean, wood-toned interior and elegantly set tables create a comfortable and refined dining experience, perfect for special occasions or intimate gatherings. The attentive, unobtrusive service further enhances the feeling of being well cared for.

properties.name: lombre378
properties.description: Step into Lombre378 and discover a haven for pastry lovers, nestled in the trendy Songridan-gil area. This bakery, offering both boulangerie and patisserie delights, has patrons returning for days on end. While it might look sweet, the quality far surpasses the price, with signature items that are a must-try. Don't miss the heart-shaped cheese mousse, a showstopper that even non-dessert lovers rave about.

properties.name: LEE JIN
properties.description:
Step into a world where dance is more than just movement; it's a career. This institution is dedicated to cultivating true dance professionals, emphasizing choreography, stage presence, teamwork, and performance direction. Learn from the best, including instructors who have worked with global sensations like BLACKPINK and appeared on 'Street Woman Fighter' (스우파). The curriculum is designed for real-world application, offering practical, hands-on classes and invaluable feedback from industry veterans.

Led by seasoned choreographers and performers, the faculty boasts an impressive roster of experience. From directing dancehall performances to backing K-pop artists and appearing on major music shows, these instructors bring a wealth of practical knowledge. Whether you're aiming to master dancehall (댄스홀) or hone your choreography (코레오그래피) skills, this is the place to elevate your artistry and prepare for a dynamic career in dance.

properties.name: Le Cochon
properties.description: Nestled on the third floor of the Arario Museum in Space, Le Cochon offers a refined French fine dining experience with a stunning view of Changdeokgung Palace. The ambiance is sophisticated yet comfortable, making it an ideal spot for a leisurely lunch or a special occasion. While the menu features a range of exquisite French dishes, the French onion soup and the scallop dishes are particularly noteworthy for their rich flavors and delicate preparation.

properties.name: Rossini
properties.description: Nestled near Anguk Station, Rossini offers a refined Italian dining experience perfect for special occasions. The ambiance is noted as excellent, making it a top recommendation for dates and celebrations. The staff provides attentive service, including helpful wine recommendations that pair beautifully with the meal. The restaurant also caters to groups, with ample seating suitable for company dinners.

properties.name: Dibs patisserie
properties.description: Dibs Patisserie is a must-visit spot in Hongdae's Yeonnam-dong, famous for its 'Dujjonku' (두쫀쿠) or Dubai chewy cookies. These treats, popularized by celebrity Kang Min-kyung, are known for their stunning visual appeal and exquisite taste. The cookies boast a wonderfully chewy exterior with a delightful crunch from kadaif (카다이프) inside, offering a rich and satisfying flavor that's surprisingly not messy. The beautiful packaging makes them a perfect gift for any special occasion.

properties.name: 딤딤섬(명동점)
properties.description:
Dim Dim Sum in Myeongdong is your go-to spot for authentic Hong Kong dim sum, a place that many travelers discover on their trips to Hong Kong. This popular chain offers a vast array of dim sum options, making it a must-visit for enthusiasts. While weekday lunchtimes can see queues of office workers, visiting on a holiday meant immediate seating. The ordering process is straightforward: check your desired items on the menu and hand it to the staff. 

The signature Crispy Cheung Fun (크리스피 창펀) is a standout, offering a delightful textural contrast. Don't miss the Eggplant Dim Sum (가지딤섬), where tender eggplant envelops a savory pork filling, drizzled with a special sauce. For a refreshing start, the Mala Cucumber (마라황과) provides a crisp, palate-cleansing bite with a mild spicy kick. Pair your meal with their smooth, aromatic Oolong Tea (우롱차), served in a unique teapot that allows for continuous brewing, perfectly complementing the rich flavors of the dim sum.

properties.name: L'enfance
properties.description: Nestled near Gyeongbokgung Station, L'enfance offers a sophisticated and clean ambiance, making it an ideal spot for dates or special occasions. The menu is extensive, catering to both lunch and dinner with distinct offerings. Standout dishes include the perfectly crispy fried olives paired with refreshing Greek yogurt, and the incredibly tender duck breast (오리 가슴살) served with a date puree, which is highly recommended as a main course. The asparagus (아스파라거스) with apple and Gruyère cheese also provides a delightful textural contrast.

properties.name: 락희안(가좌본점)
properties.description: Step into Rakheean (락희안), a venerable Chinese restaurant with a legacy stretching back to 1945. This third-generation establishment offers a refined yet trendy dining experience, perfect for family gatherings or private celebrations in its well-appointed rooms. They pride themselves on using fresh, homemade noodles and daily-changed frying oil, ensuring a wholesome meal. Don't miss their signature Daepa Ggwobarou (대파 꿔바로우), a modern take on a traditional dish, featuring crispy yet chewy pork coated in a sweet and sour sauce, elevated by the fresh bite of scallions.

properties.name: 두툼
properties.description: This spot is a must-visit for serious meat lovers, boasting a reputation as one of Seoul's top destinations for grilled pork. The star of the show is their signature samgyeopsal (삼겹살), aged to perfection in a dedicated dry-aging room, ensuring a tender and flavorful bite. The accompanying fermented anchovy sauce (meljeot, 멜젓) adds a delightful umami kick that perfectly complements the rich pork.

properties.name: LAMBSYA
properties.description: LAMBSYA offers a sophisticated yet cozy ambiance, perfect for special occasions. The star of the show is undoubtedly the lamb, with the premium French rack (프리미엄 프렌치랙) praised for its tender, juicy texture and the shoulder rack (숄더랙) offering a delightful chewiness. Don't miss their signature fried eggplant (가지튀김), a triple-fried masterpiece with a special soy sauce glaze that reviewers rave about, even overshadowing the lamb for some. The attentive service, including perfectly grilled meats and unique side dishes like the sesame rice (깨밥), elevates the dining experience.

properties.name: LA SUISSE
properties.description: Step into a charming Swiss chalet nestled in the heart of Seoul's Seochon neighborhood. La Suisse transports you with its authentic Swiss farmhouse ambiance, from the exterior adorned with Swiss flags to the cozy interior featuring warm wooden accents and picturesque scenery. It's the perfect spot for a romantic date or a special celebration, offering a unique European escape.

properties.name: la goutte
properties.description: Nestled within The Classic 500 building near Konkuk University Station, La Goutte offers a luxurious buffet experience. The interior is stunning, with a grand chandelier in the lobby and a chic, nature-inspired design featuring marble floors and natural wood accents, creating a sophisticated and serene ambiance. The spacious seating and attentive service, with staff refilling water and clearing plates promptly, enhance the overall dining pleasure. It's a perfect spot for a special family meal or a delightful escape.

properties.name: 땅코참숯구이
properties.description: This popular Seongsu-dong spot is renowned for its grilled meats, with staff expertly cooking your order right at the table. The pork neck (moksal, 목살) is a standout, described as the best the reviewer has ever tasted – incredibly juicy and flavorful. The restaurant boasts a clean, no-frills interior and attentive service, with staff even offering aprons to prevent oil splatters and providing helpful tips on the best time to photograph the perfectly grilled meat. It's a place that inspires a 300% desire to return.

properties.name: DUOMO
properties.description: Step into DUOMO, a sophisticated multi-cultural space and the first-ever B&B Italia flagship store in Korea. Spanning three floors, this showroom curated by DOUMO, a leading high-end living company, showcases the essence of luxury living. Designed by Antonio Citterio, the space itself is a masterpiece, seamlessly blending natural light, ambient lighting, and exquisite furniture to create an artistic exhibition hall where urban and natural elements converge.

properties.name: La Bonne Tarte
properties.description: La Bonne Tarte is a beloved bakery specializing in healthy tarts and breads, using organic flour and seasonal fruits for over a decade. Their signature offerings include a delightful tart set featuring nine varieties like macadamia, fig, grapefruit, blueberry, and apple. Popular choices range from the refreshing blueberry tart with its satisfying pop to the sweet and creamy banana tart. They also offer hearty breads like ciabatta and focaccia, as well as sweet treats like red bean buns and the highly sought-after ang butter bread (앙버터빵), a combination of butter and red bean paste sandwiched in ciabatta.

properties.name: THE GRIFFIN BAR
properties.description: Perched on the 11th floor of JW Marriott Dongdaemun Square Seoul, The Griffin Bar offers a sophisticated escape with breathtaking views of the city. This upscale lounge is renowned for its expertly crafted cocktails, featuring premium Diageo Reserve brands. The bar boasts three bartenders who have reached the Top 10 in the prestigious Diageo World Class competition, ensuring a world-class drinking experience. Signature drinks like the visually stunning 'Bridge of Propose' and the nuanced 'Singleton Sour' are must-tries, perfect for sharing with a loved one or enjoying a moment of refined indulgence.

properties.name: The Spot Fabulous
properties.description: Nestled near Hoehyeon Station, The Spot Fabulous is a charming cafe that stands out in the bustling Myeongdong district. This Blue Ribbon Survey-recognized spot offers a delightful escape with its beautifully presented desserts and a wide selection of drinks, including a special decaf option. The cafe boasts a spacious interior spread across two floors, though tables can be close together, the efficient staff ensures a tidy environment. It's a perfect place to unwind after exploring, offering a sweet treat with a view of the Chinese Embassy.

properties.name: 동아리(정동본점)
properties.description: Nestled on Jeongdong-gil, Dongari is a popular Japanese sukiyaki specialist that also serves as an izakaya.
